title: QA Specialist
description:
QA Specialist 



You will be the last set of eyes before anything reaches a client. If it's not good enough, it doesn't go out.



We are a UGC ad production company. We create short-form video ads for brands — the kind that look like real conversations between real people, not polished commercials. Our clients run these on Meta, TikTok, and YouTube, and they perform because they feel human.



We work fast, we care deeply about quality, and we hold a very high bar for everything that leaves our hands. That's exactly why we're hiring for this role.



???? THE ROLE



You are the quality gate.



Your job is to review scripts and finished videos before they reach clients — and make sure nothing slips through. You'll be the last set of eyes on every piece of content we produce. Your standards become our standards.



This isn't a passive role. We need someone who catches issues others miss, communicates feedback clearly and specifically, and genuinely cares whether a video feels real or feels like an ad. If you're the kind of person who notices something is off before you can even explain why — this role was built for you.



???? WHAT YOU'LL DO



Script QA



Review scripts written by copywriters before they go to production



Flag AI-language, unnatural phrasing, and compliance violations



Check hooks, CTAs, length, and formatting against brand briefs



Leave clear, actionable comments with specific fixes — not vague notes



Video QA



Apply our 5-Pass QA method to every finished video



Review acting naturalness, b-roll relevance, and subtitle accuracy



Check pacing, fluff, music levels, lip sync, and technical issues



Leave timestamped feedback on 
 so editors know exactly what to fix



Raw Footage Review



Review raw footage to identify the best and most natural takes



Timestamp approved clips so editors know exactly what to use



Note off-script takes, backup options, and anything substandard



Communication & Standards



Communicate feedback to editors, copywriters, and clients professionally



Hold the quality line even under deadline pressure



Help maintain and improve our internal QA standards over time



? WHAT 'GOOD' LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE



? You watch a video and immediately feel when something is 'off' — even before you can articulate why



? You know the difference between a line that sounds natural and a line that sounds like it was written by ChatGPT



? Your feedback is specific: not "this feels unnatural" but "at 0:42 the actor pauses to read their phone — swap for take 2"



? You don't approve something just because a deadline is close — you flag it and propose a solution



? You can handle a client pushing back on your notes and hold your position calmly and professionally



? You watch top-performing ads and understand exactly why they worked



???? YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:



A sharp eye for performance and naturalness in video



Experience reviewing or editing short-form video content (UGC, ads, social media)



Strong written communication — your notes are clear, not vague



Comfort working with tools like 
, Google Docs, and Notion



The ability to manage your own time and hit turnaround windows without hand-holding



Genuine pride in quality — you care that the work is actually good



Bonus points if you have:



A background in video production, casting, or creative direction



Experience working with UGC creators or performance ad agencies



Familiarity with Meta Ads or TikTok ad formats



An understanding of what makes a scroll-stopping hook



Experience in copywriting or script review



???? THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF...



? You approve content just to get it off your plate



? You give vague feedback that editors can't act on



? You can't handle direct conversations about quality



? You think "it's mostly fine" is good enough



? You need constant hand-holding to know what needs fixing



? You confuse 'polished' with 'natural' — they are not the same thing



???? THE STANDARD WE HOLD



Natural beats polished. Every time.



Every video we produce needs to feel like a candid conversation — the kind people actually have. Not a sales pitch. Not a script. A real 
nt between two people, one of whom happens to know about a product.



When you QA our work, you are protecting that standard. If a line sounds like it was written by AI, it gets fixed. If an actor is clearly reading off their phone, the take gets swapped. If b-roll doesn't match the story, it gets replaced.



We watch every piece of content at least five times before it leaves us. That's not obsessive — that's the job.



???? TOOLS YOU'LL WORK WITH




 · Google Docs · Notion · 5-Pass QA Method · Script & Video QA · Raw Footage Review · Subtitle Review · Timestamp Feedback · Compliance Checking · Async Workflow



???? HOW TO APPLY



We hire on results, not CVs.



To apply, you'll complete a short trial task that mirrors exactly what this job involves — QA-ing a script, reviewing a finished video, timestamping raw footage, analysing top-performing content, and working through a few real client scenarios.



This is how we see how you actually think. And it's how you'll know whether this work is something you're genuinely great at.

title: Sourcing Agent - native-level Mandarin
description:
Full-Time | Remote | USD $1,500/month



Promo Punks is an Australian promotional products company on a mission to find the most creative, unique, and high-quality promotional products coming out of Asia. We need someone with sharp sourcing instincts and native-level Mandarin to be our eyes, ears, and voice on the ground.



This isn't order-chasing. You'll be actively hunting for standout products and building relationships with factories and suppliers that most competitors will never find.



What you'll do:



- Research, identify, and reach out to new suppliers and manufacturers across China and greater Asia

- Negotiate pricing, MOQs, lead times, and terms on behalf of Promo Punks

- Vet suppliers for quality, reliability, and production capability

- Continuously scout for innovative, creative, and trending promotional products that stand out from the usual catalogue junk

- Manage ongoing supplier communication and relationships in Mandarin

- Coordinate samples, pricing sheets, and product specs for our team in Australia

- Monitor industry trends, trade shows, and sourcing platforms (1688, Alibaba, Made-in-China, etc.) to keep our product offering fresh

- Flag quality or compliance issues early and help resolve them



Skills & experience required:



- Native or fluent Mandarin speaker — you can negotiate confidently with Chinese suppliers in their language

- Strong English communication skills — you'll be reporting to and collaborating with our Australian team daily

- Proven experience in product sourcing, procurement, or supply chain — ideally in promotional products, gifting, merchandise, or consumer goods

- A sharp eye for creative and unique products — you know the difference between generic and genuinely impressive

- Confident negotiator who can push for better pricing and terms without damaging relationships

- Highly organised — you can manage multiple suppliers, quotes, and sample requests simultaneously

- Self-starter who takes initiative and doesn't wait to be told what to find next



Nice to have:



- Existing network of suppliers or factory contacts in China

- Experience with trade shows like Canton Fair, Global Sources, or Yiwu markets

- Familiarity with product compliance and import requirements for Australia

- Experience using sourcing platforms like 1688, Alibaba, or Made-in-China professionally



Compensation:



USD $1,500 per month, full-time. We're based in Australia (Brisbane, AEST) so reasonable timezone overlap for daily communication is expected.



To apply:



Tell us about a time you found an outstanding or unexpected product through your sourcing efforts — what made it special and how did you find it. Include a brief summary of your sourcing experience and any supplier networks you bring to the table.

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview: 



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office. 



 

Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents. 



 

About Power Plus Systems: 



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission. 



 

Objectives: 

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.



 

Competencies: 

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.



 

Equipment Requirements:  

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.



 

Physical Requirements: 

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

 

Benefits: 

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply: 



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below. 




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply. 



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

title: B2B Lead Generation Specialist — US Commercial (Part-time)
description:
About us



We're a fast-growing wellness brand expanding our commercial footprint across the United States and Australia. Our products are used in high-performance recovery, hospitality, and wellness environments. We have strong direct-to-consumer momentum and are now building out our B2B commercial channel — and we need someone experienced to help lead that charge.



The opportunity



This is not a hand-holding role. We're looking for someone who has done this before — built lead gen infrastructure, identified the right contacts, and driven consistent pipeline for B2B sales teams. You'll be working across a small number of target sectors in the US and will have significant ownership over how we approach the market.



Target sectors



You'll be generating leads across a focused set of commercial verticals including:

- Wellness centres and recovery studios

- Hospitality (boutique hotels, resorts, members clubs)

- Universities, colleges, and high schools

- Fire departments and law enforcement

- New residential developments and building amenities



What you'll own



- Building and managing a lead generation infrastructure from the ground up

- Researching and building high-quality contact lists by sector

- Setting up and managing outreach tools and sending infrastructure

- Running multi-channel outreach campaigns 
 + LinkedIn)

- Qualifying inbound responses and flagging warm leads for the team

- Building a simple reporting structure so we always know what's working



What we're looking for



- Proven experience running B2B lead generation campaigns independently

- Strong working knowledge of lead gen and outreach tools (we want to hear what you use)

- Comfortable working with a small, fast-moving team with limited hand-holding

- Excellent written English — you'll be representing our brand in outbound communications

- Self-directed — you manage your own workflow and proactively report on progress

- Experience targeting US-based contacts is a strong plus



Engagement



This is a part-time remote role with flexible hours. We care about results, not clock-watching. If this goes well, there's a clear path to expanded scope as the commercial channel grows.



How to apply



Please apply with:



1. A brief overview of a B2B lead gen campaign you've run — what sectors, what tools, what results

2. The tools you currently use or have used for list building, outreach, and tracking

3. Your availability and preferred working arrangement



We review applications on a rolling basis. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a short practical task before any interviews.

title: Automation Specialist (n8n + AI Workflows)
description:
About Us



We are looking for a full-time Automation Specialist for our YouTube channels:



- 

- 



We make documentary-style videos that explain complex stories in a clear and engaging way. Behind every video is a sophisticated, partially automated production pipeline, and we're looking for someone to own, maintain, and scale it.



About the Role



You will be the technical owner of our automation infrastructure. We run a self-hosted OpenClaw instance (open-source AI agent framework) alongside a self-hosted n8n instance that powers our production workflows. Our Notion workspace serves as the central hub for the entire team, covering project management, status tracking, and automation triggers.



Your job is to deeply understand our existing systems, keep them reliable, and work with the team to expand what they can do.





What You'll Do



• Own and maintain our OpenClaw agent infrastructure and n8n workflows, keeping everything running reliably, debugging failures, and documenting what's in place

• Own our Notion infrastructure, maintaining and improving the structure of our Notion workspace, databases, views, and templates so it stays clean, consistent, and usable as the team grows

• Collaborate with the production team to identify new automation opportunities across the pipeline: research, scripting, asset management, editing handoffs, publishing, and reporting

• Build Notion-triggered automation systems, where a status change or button click in Notion fires off a chain of background automations

• Optimize existing automations for efficiency and cost by reviewing current workflows to reduce unnecessary API calls, LLM usage, and compute costs while maintaining performance

• Improve system reliability and scalability over time through better error handling, fallback logic, monitoring, and documentation

• Explore AI-assisted automation by extending our existing LLM-powered workflows with new capabilities such as research assistance, script support, and performance reporting



Our Stack



• Automation: n8n (self-hosted), OpenClaw (self-hosted AI agent framework)

• Project management: Notion (extensive workflow, partially automated)

• Communication & storage: Slack, Google Docs, Dropbox

• APIs in use: YouTube API, Notion API, Google Workspace, Dropbox,

• Production tools (used by the creative team): Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe After Effects, Blender



Required Skills



• Proficiency in n8n, including building, debugging, and maintaining complex multi-step workflows

• Experience with OpenClaw or similar open-source AI agent frameworks

• Comfortable working with APIs (REST, webhooks, OAuth, environment variables)

• Experience integrating LLMs into real production pipelines

• Solid Linux server administration basics (cron, systemd, logs, SSH)

• Strong troubleshooting mindset, able to diagnose and fix broken automations independently



Preferred Skills



• Experience with browser automation such as scraping dashboards or web interfaces without APIs

• Familiarity with Notion API and database automation

• Experience with Slack bot integrations and workflow notifications

• Knowledge of Google Workspace APIs (Docs, Drive, Sheets, YouTube Analytics)

• Experience managing multi-agent AI systems with scheduling and memory

• Background in video or media production environments is a plus



Working Hours



This is a remote role. You must be available for a minimum of 6 hours overlap with Cyprus business hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EEST (UTC+3). Suitable timezones include most of Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and parts of South/Southeast Asia.



Compensation



$1,200 USD/month, paid monthly via Wise.



To Apply



Please send:



• Your CV

• A brief description of 2–3 automations you're proud of, including what problem they solved and what tools you used

• Any links to workflows, GitHub repos, or portfolios (optional but appreciated)

title: Procurement Engineer
description:
Procurement Manager – TSC Supply (International Role)



Location: Papua New Guinea / China (Rotational Overseas Placement)

Roster: 2 months on, 1 month off

Salary: USD $900 – $1,600 per month (based on experience)



About the Role



The Sourcing Co (TSC Supply) is seeking a driven and technically minded Procurement Manager to join our growing international team.



This role sits at the intersection of construction, design, and global manufacturing, making it ideal for professionals from backgrounds such as:

Mechanical Engineers, Structural Engineers, Architects, Draftsmen, Quantity Surveyors, and Interior Designers.



You will be responsible for managing end-to-end procurement across a wide range of building and fit-out products, working closely with clients, project teams, and our China-based operations.



If you enjoy working on real projects, solving technical challenges, and being part of a global supply chain, this is a unique opportunity to step into an international career.



What You’ll Be Doing

Client & Project Coordination

Work directly with clients to understand project requirements, timelines, and specifications

Review and interpret drawings, BOQs, and technical documentation

Clarify materials, finishes, and compliance requirements

Provide practical input and value-engineering solutions

Procurement & Sourcing

Joinery, cabinetry, and furniture

Prefabricated and modular structures

Light steel framing and building materials

Custom-manufactured items

Factory & China Team Coordination

Quality, Logistics & Delivery

Coordinate with logistics teams to ensure timely delivery

Track shipments, documentation, and compliance

Assist in resolving production or delivery issues

Product Development

Learn and work across a wide range of materials and construction systems

Support new product sourcing and supplier development

Contribute to improving procurement processes and efficiencies

What We’re Looking For

Essential

Experience in procurement, project coordination, or technical sourcing

Ability to read and understand technical drawings and plans

Strong communication and coordination skills

High attention to detail and problem-solving ability

Ability to manage multiple projects

Preferred

Background in engineering, construction, or design

(Mechanical, Structural, Civil, Architecture, Drafting, QS, Interior Design)

Experience with offshore manufacturing (especially China)

Knowledge of building materials, joinery, or prefabrication

Exposure to PNG or international project environments

Who You Are

Practical, solutions-focused, and proactive

Keen to learn and expand across different product categories

Comfortable working across cultures and international teams

Takes ownership and thrives in a fast-paced environment

Open to travel and working abroad

Travel & Lifestyle

Rotational overseas role: 2 months on, 1 month off

Travel to China (head office & factories) and Papua New Guinea (projects)

A unique opportunity to combine technical work with international exposure

Why Join The Sourcing Co

Work on real projects across construction, fit-out, and manufacturing

Gain hands-on experience with global supply chains

Exposure to a wide range of products and industries

Clear pathway for growth within the group

title: Part-Time Brand Protection Specialist (Meta Ad Moderator)
description:
The Role

We are looking for a vigilant, detail-oriented Part-Time Virtual Assistant to serve as the front-line defense for our advertising campaigns. Your mission is to maintain a "clean," high-converting environment across all active
 and Instagram ads by managing social proof and neutralizing negative interference.  This is a long-term, part-time position (approx. 10–20 hours per week) designed for someone who is highly organized and can provide consistent "patrols" throughout the day.  



What You'll Do

-Ad Shielding: Perform regular patrols of all active Meta ads to hide spam, "scam" claims, and offensive language

-Strategic Hiding: Identify and hide price-shaming comments or competitor links that steal our traffic

-Customer Advocacy: Identify legitimate customer concerns or product questions and flag them to the team via 
.  

-Social Proof Boosting: Like positive comments and heart customer testimonials to increase engagement and algorithm favorability

-Daily Reporting: Provide quick "All Clear" updates or highlight recurring negative themes in team channels



What We're Looking For

-Eagle Eye: Obsessive attention to detail—nothing slips past you

-Reliable: Since ads run 24/7, we need someone who is consistent with their "check-in" times.  

-Discerning: You know the difference between a troll (hide immediately) and a frustrated customer (needs help)

-Strong English: High-level proficiency to ensure you represent the brand voice correctly



Bonus Points:

-Experience moderating e-commerce or DTC ad comments

-Background in customer service or community management

-Understanding of how social proof impacts ad performance

-You've worked with high-spend ad accounts before



Our Tech Stack 

-To be successful in this role, you must be proficient in:  

-Meta Business Suite: For all comment moderation and engagement.  

-ClickUp: For task management, following SOPs, and logging your shifts.  

-
: For real-time team communication and reporting urgent issues.  



This Role Is NOT For...

We're being direct because we respect your time:

-People who check in once a day and call it done

-Those who can't tell the difference between a troll and a real customer

-Anyone who hides everything without thinking

-People who miss details or let things slip through

title: Automation/Scraping Engineer
description:

About Rescale Media

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives.

We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 30+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started.

dia



Our Core Values

ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.

TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Mission

Continuously ingest fresh external signals.

You'll build the systems that keep our data pipelines fed with fresh, clean, reliable data from the outside world—no matter how hard sources try to stop you.



What You'll Do

Build and maintain browser automation systems

Normalize and clean data for downstream consumption

Implement resilience patterns—retries, proxies, rate limiting

Ensure data freshness with monitoring and alerting

What We're Looking For

2+ years of experience with web scraping and automation

Strong experience with Playwright, Puppeteer, or Selenium

Understanding of anti-bot detection and evasion techniques

Experience with proxy management and rotation

Proficiency in Python or Node.js

Familiarity with HTML parsing and data extraction



Bonus Points:

Background with headless browsers at scale

You've dealt with CAPTCHAs, fingerprinting, and other anti-bot measures

Experience with distributed scraping infrastructure



This Role Is NOT For...

We're being direct because we respect your time:

Engineers who write scrapers that break when a class name changes

Those who haven't dealt with anti-bot systems

Anyone who thinks scraping is just BeautifulSoup + requests

title: WE’RE HIRING: Video Editor for a Fast-Growing Supplement Brand*
description:
The Role



You're editing direct response video ads that sell. Not brand films. Not vlogs. Not cinematic transitions with mood lighting. Ads that stop the scroll, hold attention, and make people buy.



You'll take written ad briefs from our Creative Director and Copywriters and bring them to life — sourcing the right footage, syncing visuals to voiceover, pacing every cut for maximum impact, and making sure the final product looks native, not produced.



The smallest details make the biggest differences. A wrong clip kills the hook. A slow transition loses her. A misplaced caption buries the message. You obsess over these details because they're the difference between an ad shown to millions and one that dies at $50 spend.



---



## What You'll Do



**Onboarding:**



- Learn our brand guidelines, tone, and visual style

- Study our winning ads and understand exactly why they work

- Understand our avatar — who we're talking to, what she feels, why she stops scrolling



**Ongoing:**



- Edit 15–20 ad briefs per week

- Source relevant B-roll from YouTube, stock sites, and other platforms — find the RIGHT clips that match the script and hook, not random footage

- Use AI tools (image generation, video generation, voiceover AI, background removal, etc.) to speed up production and enhance creative quality

- Upload and organize edited ads into specific folders with correct resolutions (1080×1080, 1080×1920, 1920×1080)

- Communicate with copywriters and Creative Director to ensure ad concepts are executed correctly

- Listen to feedback and apply it — for existing ads and new ones

- Learn our Ads Strategy to understand what makes a "winner" vs a "loser"

- Complete a weekly 30-minute ad analysis and review on concepts you've created



---



## What You Need To Know



- How to source footage strategically (YouTube, Pexels, Storyblocks, etc.)

- How to match visuals to voiceover — intentional clips, not random B-roll

- Pacing for direct response ads — fast, punchy, no dead space

- Text and caption placement that's readable and scroll-stopping

- Basic color correction and audio cleanup

- Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or CapCut

- AI tools for creative production — image generators (Midjourney, Freepik, Leonardo, Ideogram), AI voiceover tools (ElevenLabs or similar), AI video tools (Runway, Pika, Kling, etc.), and background removal/upscaling tools. You don't need to be an expert in all of them, but you need to be comfortable using AI to move faster and produce better work.



---



## Qualifications



- 1+ year editing video ads (not vlogs, not YouTube videos — ads)

- 1+ year working with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

- Experience editing eCommerce or direct response ads specifically

- Comfortable using AI tools for image generation, voiceover, video generation, and creative enhancement

- Experience working in a team environment and understanding individual roles



---



## Character Traits



- Excruciatingly high attention to detail with every edit

- Artistic and creative-oriented mindset

- Visual communicator and storyteller

- High curiosity and an active imagination

- Strong willingness to do the "boring" work required

- Great communicator — responsive, clear, no guesswork

- Time-efficient, but willing to spend extra time to get it right

- Coachable with a genuine desire to grow your skill set



---



## This Is NOT For You If:



- You are not a creative person

- You don't enjoy the editing process

- You think "creative" means slow transitions and mood lighting

- You don't like a high-operating, fast-feedback environment

- You're unorganized or miss deadlines

- You can't take direct feedback

- You don't understand direct response or eCommerce

- You refuse to learn or use AI tools



---



## What We Provide



- All scripts written for you — you focus on execution

- Clear direction on what visuals we need for every brief

- Stock footage subscriptions and tools

- AI tool access and resources

- Fast feedback loops so you improve quickly

- Long-term work if you're good

- A team that actually knows what converts



---



## Growth Potential



We value growth and development. With strong performance, your path as a Video Editor includes:



- Priority on editing ads — you get the bulk of requests

- Higher pay as you prove yourself

- Creative Director role — lead and manage other editors, focus on high-level strategy



---



## How To Apply



Send us:



1. Links to 2–3 direct response video ads you've edited (not brand videos, not vlogs — ads that sell a product)

2. A 2-sentence explanation of why each ad works (what makes it convert, not just "looks good")

3. A short example of how you've used any AI tool in your editing workflow (screenshot, before/after, or just describe it)

4. Your availability and hourly rate



Subject line:** "Elixir Video Editor — I edit ads that convert

title: Dropshipping Expert (Remote Ecommerce Manager)
description:
E-commerce Manager (DFY) — Full-time, Remote

We build and run online stores for clients (investors).

Your job: take a store from zero to launch in 60 days, test fast, scale what works, change what doesn’t.

What you’ll do

• Find products to test (quick research, clear angles).

• Build the store (Shopify pages, basic upsells, trust basics).

• Make simple creatives (images/UGC, short clips, basic edits—speed over perfection).

• Run ads (mainly Meta): launch, test, kill losers, scale winners.

• Iterate fast: if a product’s first data is bad, pause and swap the product (keep the same store).

• Report simply: short Loom updates + a quick call notes what was tested, what’s next.

We focus on what moves ROAS. No extra “nice to have” marketing unless it helps sales.

How we work (60-day sprint)

• Weeks 1–2: Pick 3–5 products, build store v1, create creatives, launch tests.

• Weeks 3–4: Cut weak tests, change creatives/offers, improve product page, try new audiences.

• Weeks 5–8: Scale what works, add new creatives, simple LTV boosts (bundles/upsells), keep documenting.

Expectations

• Do the maximum reasonable work in 60 days.

• If it works ? we scale.

• If it doesn’t ? be transparent about everything tested.

What we provide/allow

• Each client has their own ad account (no overlap issues).

• If you find a winner, you may test the same product for multiple clients (different audiences/ad accounts).

• Tools: Shopify, Meta Ads, basic creative tools (Canva/CapCut), Sheets, Slack, Loom.

What you need

• Real DFY/agency experience: product research + store build + creatives + Meta ads.

• Comfortable killing bad tests fast and swapping products.

• Clear English, short Looms, simple written updates.

• Full-time availability (you may handle multiple clients at once).

Pay & growth

• Base: $150/month per client ($300 per client per 2-month sprint).

• Typical load: 15–20 clients ? e.g. $300 × 20 = $6,000 per 2 months (base).

• Bonuses:

o $100 for each usable video case-study interview you help create.

o Performance bonuses (examples): $200 if a store hits $10k in 30 days; $500 if $50k in 90 days.

o Profit share: 5–10% of client profits (usual baseline 10% when applicable).

o Renewal/DWY assist bonus if you actively help the salesperson secure a renewal (e.g., you record a call advising the client to renew).

• Career path: manage more clients, earn more base + bonuses; grow into DFY/Delivery Manager with extra fixed pay.

How we measure success

• Speed to first launch (goal: within 7–10 days of brief).

• Number of tests/iterations each week.

• Smart decisions (pause early losers, double down on winners).

• Improving unit economics (CPM/CTR/CVR/CPA/ROAS).

• Happy, informed clients (clear Looms + on-time check-ins).



How to apply (simple)

 
  with subject “DFY Ecom Manager – Your Name” and include:

1. 3 links to stores or campaigns you ran (1–2 lines of results each).

2. 2–3 screenshots of ad setups/results (blur sensitive data) + what you’d try next.

3. A 2–3 min Loom: show a test you stopped or scaled, and why.

4. Your start date and working hours/time zone.

________________________________________

You ship. You test. You iterate. You communicate.

If that’s your style, we want you.

title: Ecommerce manager for Done For You info business offer
description:
E-commerce Manager (DFY) — Product Research · Store Build · Creatives · Ads



Full-time · Remote 



About the Role



We run a high-velocity Done-For-You (DFY) e-commerce program for investor-type clients. You will own the end-to-end build and launch of stores and campaigns over a 60-day sprint: product research ? store setup ? creative production ? launch ? ad management ? iteration. The goal is simple: do the maximum reasonable work in 60 days to validate and scale winners—or transparently document tests when they don’t meet standards.



What You’ll Own (End-to-End)



Product Research: Rapid validation, competitive angles, pricing, light PMF checks.



If a product underperforms early (poor first-data), pause and switch—no ad-spend waste.



Allowed to test one winner across multiple students (different ad accounts/audiences).



Store Building: Fast, clean, conversion-ready storefronts (home/product/cart/checkout), upsells, trust, policy pages.



Creative Production: Functional > perfect. Use AI and templates to ship iterative UGC, hooks, statics, short-form, variations for fast testing.



Media Buying: Launch, structure, and manage campaigns; budgets, targeting, CBO/ABO, retargeting, bid strategies; daily/48h optimization cycles focused on ROAS-impacting inputs.



Reporting & Communication: Looms + update calls, clear metrics, decisions, next steps. Transparent logs of tests and outcomes.



How We Work (Principles)



ROAS first: No “nice-to-have” busywork (e.g., organic social) unless it affects revenue.



Speed & iteration: Ship ? measure ? change product/creative swiftly; keep the store.



Documentation: Short Looms and checklists for every major change.



Transparency: If it works ? scale. If not ? show exactly what was tested.



Your First 60 Days (Typical)



Week 1–2: Research 3–5 products, build store v1, produce creative packs v1, launch test campaigns.



Week 3–4: Kill underperformers, refactor creatives, iterate offers/angles, tighten PDP, expand audiences.



Week 5–8: Scale validated SKU(s), add creative v2/v3, deepen LTV (bundles/upsells/email basics), document everything.



Success Metrics (You’ll Be Measured On)



Speed to First Data: Time to launch from brief (target: ?7–10 days).



Testing Throughput: # of creative/product iterations per week.



Decision Quality: Timely pauses/switches on poor data; clear rationale.



Unit Economics: Progression in CPR/CTR/CVR/CPA/ROAS during the 60-day window.



Client Experience: Update cadence (Looms/calls), clarity, and renewal propensity.



Requirements (Must-Have)



Hands-on DFY/agency experience across product research, store build, creatives, and paid social (Meta/TikTok/Google—Meta required).



Performance mindset: Comfortable killing ideas quickly and switching SKUs.



Tooling: Shopify, Meta Ads Manager, Google Analytics, product spy tools, creative editors (CapCut/Premiere/Canva), and at least one AI workflow (scripts/voiceover/image).



Communication: Clear English, concise Looms, structured weekly updates.



Availability: Full-time; able to manage multiple accounts concurrently.



Nice to Have



Background in UGC pipelines, briefs, and influencer seeding.



Experience with offer tests (pricing, bundles, urgency, guarantees).



Knowledge of
/SMS basics (flows for post-purchase/abandon) as revenue assists.



Familiarity with CBO/Advantage+ Shopping, creative exhaustion detection, and bid caps.



Compensation & Upside



Base per client: $150/month per customer ? $300 per customer for a 2-month sprint.



Typical load: 15–20 customers simultaneously ? example: $300 × 20 = $6,000 per 2 months (base).



Bonuses:



$100 for a video interview (case study) you help produce.



Performance bonuses (examples): $200 for hitting $10k in 30 days; $500 for $50k in 90 days.



Profit share: 5–10% of the customer’s profits (usual baseline 10%), when applicable.



Renewal assist bonus: % bonus if you concretely assist the seller to secure a renewal or DWY (e.g., you run/record a consult that motivates the client to renew).



Growth path:



Service renewals ? increased portfolio / higher base.



DFY/Delivery Manager Rep track with additional fixed monthly compensation.



Expanded profit-share on accounts you scale.



Important: Covering both roles (store + ads) is full-time. Each client runs on independent ad accounts; audience overlap is managed in testing.



Tools You’ll Use



Shopify · Meta Ads Manager · TikTok Ads (nice) · Google Ads (nice) · GA4 · Product research tools (AdSpy/Pipiads/Ecomhunt or similar) · CapCut/Premiere/Canva · Sheets/Docs · Slack · Loom.



Schedule & Collaboration



Remote. Flexible hours, but you must maintain daily ad checks and scheduled client updates.



Weekly ops call, async Slack, and shared trackers.



You’ll maintain a clear testing log per client.



What Success Looks Like (90 Days)



You’re running multiple client sprints with on-time launches and disciplined test cadences.



You can diagnose quickly (creative fatigue vs offer vs PDP vs audience vs price).



You’ve produced case-study-worthy results OR clean documentation showing rigorous tests and next steps.



Your client renewals and referral rates are trending up.



How to Apply



 

] with the subject “DFY E-commerce Manager — Your Name” and include:

title: Media Buyer
description:
Hello Potential New Hire!!



Welcome to Kind Hearted Company.



We're looking for a Meta Ads Specialist 
 + Instagram) to help us build our growth system.

We have some structure in place but need someone to take this by the reins. Someone who knows the job end to end.



You can learn more about Kind Hearted Company at:





We’re hiring a Meta Ads Specialist to plan, build, and run performance campaigns end-to-end and that means creative, funnels, tracking, optimization, and scaling.



This role is for someone who can work independently, take ownership, and drive outcomes without constant direction.



What makes this role different

We offer 25+ home and commercial services. That means your edge isn’t only ad setup,  it’s understanding how to market each service clearly and then combine/bundle services in a way that increases reach and improves ROI.



What you’ll do

Plan campaigns and offers (service-by-service and bundled offers)

Build and manage Meta campaigns (audiences, testing, scaling, retargeting)

Create ad creatives (static + copy; video is a bonus)

Build WordPress funnels (landing pages, forms, conversion flow)

Work with our CRM and copy existing integrations (then improve them)

Track and report performance with clear weekly updates and action steps



Skills we’re looking for:

Strong Meta Ads experience (proof required)

Creative ability (simple design + strong messaging)

WordPress funnel building experience

CRM experience + integrations



Comfortable working across multiple service categories



The most important trait: honesty

If you’re missing a skill, tell us. We’ll choose an honest person with real potential over someone who oversells.



Required Skills:

(Please do not apply if you are missing experience in the below, we will test you in your interview to understand if you have the skillset)

Creative planning

Understanding different levels of marketing and customer

Copy writing and title (AB testing)

Creating wordpress landing pages 

Integrating with Zoho CRM (We have an integration you can copy)

End to end testing



Compensation

We pay based on value and results, not job titles.

Expect to start at a lower rate during the initial period while we confirm fit and performance. If you deliver measurable outcomes, your rate will increase.



Bonus

13th-month bonus paid mid-December (based on agreed monthly compensation).



How to apply

Please include:



A short intro

Campaign examples + results (screenshots are fine)

Samples of creatives/funnels (if available)

Your starting monthly rate + the rate you expect once performing strongly



We're looking for a specialist to work with our SEO specialist and who is available on PST 8 hours a day, 5 days a week.

We are looking forward to hearing from you! You are more likely to get the job if you're willing to demonstrate the ability to get results before you ask for a large salary. You can earn an unlimited amount at Kind Hearted, but we do things the right way here where you show your skills first. 



Thank you!

title: Recruitment Operations Specialist (US Hours)
description:
We are a growing recruitment and talent operations company working with multiple US-based offices. We are looking for an experienced recruitment operations specialist to help manage high volumes of candidates across multiple applicant tracking systems (ATS).



This role is ideal for someone with recruitment experience who enjoys working with systems, moving candidates through pipelines, and ensuring interviews are fully booked and attended.



You will be handling high candidate volume (1,000+ per day) and supporting the recruitment flow from application to interview booking.



Key Responsibilities:

• Managing multiple Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS)

• Responding to large volumes of candidate messages (SMS,
, ATS inbox)

• Moving candidates through the call list and interview stages

• Booking candidates into interviews

• Sending confirmation and reminder messages

• Following up with candidates who do not respond

• Ensuring strong interview show rates

• Keeping candidate records updated and accurate

• Supporting daily recruitment operations



Requirements:

- Fluent English (written and spoken)

- Previous recruitment or staffing experience

- Experience using ATS platforms (any system is fine)

- Comfortable working with high volume (1000+ candidates daily)

- Strong attention to detail

- Fast and accurate typing

- Professional and clear communication

- Able to work independently

- Reliable internet and computer

- Able to work US business hours



Working Hours:

• Full-time

• US business hours (EST/CST)

• Long-term position



Why Join Us?

• Stable long-term role

• Work with a professional international team

• Clear processes and training provided

• Opportunity to grow into senior operations roles

• Performance-based pay increases



To Apply:

Please include in your application:

A short summary of your recruitment experience

Which ATS systems you have used

Your internet speed

Your headset type

Your availability for US hours

A short voice recording (optional but highly preferred)

title: Full-Time Recruitment Admin / CRM Operations Specialist
description:
This role requires full-time commitment. Applicants who are currently working another job, freelancing full-time, or managing multiple long-term contracts should not apply.



Location: Remote 

Hours: Full-Time (40 hours/week)

Schedule: Aligned with US business hours (EST/CST coverage required)

Start: Immediate



About the Role

We are hiring a top-tier full-time Recruitment Admin to support a fast-growing international recruitment operation serving U.S.-based offices.

This is not a basic VA role.

You will be responsible for managing high-volume candidate communication, CRM systems,
 inboxes, and daily operational workflows that directly impact hiring results. We are looking for someone who is highly organised, fast, detail-driven, and takes full ownership of outcomes.

This role suits someone who wants to be a key operational performer, not just complete tasks.



Core Responsibilities

- Candidate Communication & Inbox Management

- Reply promptly and professionally to candidate SMS, WhatsApp, and CR
ssages

- Clear and manage shared
 inboxes daily

- Ensure candidates receive accurate, timely responses and next-step instructions

- Maintain a professional, friendly, and brand-appropriate tone at all times

- CRM & Recruitment Systems



Confidently use CRM systems to:

- Update candidate statuses

- Tag, move, and progress candidates through pipelines

- Log notes and outcomes accurately

- Process daily call lists and assign them correctly

- Ensure no candidate is missed or left uncontacted

- Call List & Workflow Processing

- Prepare, clean, and organise daily call lists

- Ensure lists are accurate, prioritised, and ready for callers

- Track outcomes and feed results back into the CRM



Operational Support

-Support recruiters and callers with admin tasks that improve efficiency

- Flag system issues, broken links, or workflow gaps proactively

- Follow internal SOPs precisely while also identifying improvements

- Maintain clean, structured systems at all times



What “Top-Level Performance” Looks Like

- Messages replied to quickly and accurately

- Inbox stays clean and organised

- CRM is always up to date and reliable

- Callers never wait on lists or information

- Candidates have a smooth, professional experience

- Mistakes are rare — ownership is high



Required Skills & Experience

You must have:

- Proven experience as a Recruitment Admin, Operations Admin, or Senior VA

- Strong CRM experience (any major system — training provided on ours)

- Excellent written English (clear, professional, confident)

- High attention to detail and accuracy

- Ability to work independently and manage priorities

- Confidence working in fast-paced, high-volume environments



Nice to have (but not required):

- Experience supporting US-based recruitment or sales teams

- Familiarity with SMS/email automation tools

- KPI-driven or performance-tracked roles previously



Personality Fit

We are looking for someone who is:

- Proactive, not reactive

- Organised and structured

- Calm under pressure

- Accountable and reliable

- Hungry to perform at a high level consistently

- This role is best suited to someone who takes pride in being excellent at operations.



Compensation & Growth

- Competitive full-time salary (based on experience)

-Long-term role with stability

- Opportunity to grow into senior operations or team lead positions

- Training, systems, and clear expectations provided



How to Apply (IMPORTANT)

To apply, please include:

- A short introduction about yourself

- Your experience with CRM systems

- An example of a role where accuracy and speed mattered

- Your internet speed and home setup

Applications without these details will not be considered.

title: Fintech Senior Account Manager
description:
This is a high-impact role designed for a leader who can manage high-level Fintech client accounts while simultaneously overseeing internal performance and strategy. You will manage our most significant accounts (like AI Acquisitions) and ensure the team is hitting their KPIs. You are the "standard-bearer" for the company, ensuring strategic account planning, revenue growth & sales, product & technical liaison for Fintech solutions, client relationship management, performance monitoring, and contract negotiation.



Key Responsibilities:

High-Level Account Strategy: Lead the onboarding and management of our largest partner accounts, ensuring they achieve maximum funding ROI.

Operational Oversight (HR): Manage team expectations, monitor KPIs, and conduct performance reviews to ensure everyone is operating at a high standard.

Conflict Resolution: Step in to handle complex client issues or internal bottlenecks with a "business-first" mentality.

Process Standardization: Work with Leadership to implement and enforce standardized operating procedures (SOPs) across the account management team.

Direct Leadership: Act as a mentor to junior Account Managers and Loan Processors, ensuring the "Pod" structure is functioning efficiently.



Who You Are:

A High-Standard Leader: You are not a "cookie-cutter" HR person. You are someone who holds a high bar for performance and isn't afraid to have direct, professional conversations.

Strategically Minded: You can see the "leaky buckets" in a business and know how to plug them through better management.

Senior Energy: You can hold your own in a conversation with C-suite partners and direct them on what they need to do to succeed.



Requirements:

5+ years of experience in Senior Account Management, Operations, or HR Leadership in a remote setting.

Deep experience with the US market and/or Financial Services/Lending.

Expert-level communication and organizational skills.

Ability to manage a remote team and protect the interests of the business.

title: Sales Representative — Car Dealer Outreach
description:
ShiftHaul is a growing auto transport brokerage operating across the United States. We connect car dealerships, auction houses, and private clients with reliable carrier networks to move vehicles safely and on time. Our mission is to deliver fast, transparent, and hassle-free vehicle shipping



   We are looking for a driven and results-oriented Sales Representative to grow our dealer client base. Your primary role is to identify and contact auto dealerships across the US via outbound cold calls, present ShiftHaul's services, and convert prospects into repeat customers. This is a high-activity, high-reward sales position.



Key Responsibilities

• Make 80–120 outbound cold calls per day to franchised and independent auto dealerships

• Identify decision-makers (fleet managers, GMs, used car directors) and build relationships

• Educate prospects on ShiftHaul's services, pricing, and turnaround times

• Follow up with leads using CRM tools; maintain accurate records of all interactions

• Close initial orders and transition accounts to ongoing dispatch flow

• Meet or exceed monthly sales targets (number of new accounts + shipped orders)

• Collaborate with dispatch team to ensure smooth onboarding of new dealer clients

• Stay current on industry trends, competitor pricing, and dealer needs

Requirements

• Minimum 6 month of experience in auto transport brokerage or dealership sales

• Solid understanding of the US auto transport market (lanes, pricing, seasonality)

• Strong phone communication skills — comfortable making high-volume outbound calls

• Experience with CRM systems (Super Dispatch, AMS, HubSpot, or similar)

• Self-motivated, goal-driven, and able to work independently in a remote environment

• Need to know English. 

• Reliable internet connection and personal computer

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview: 



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office. 



 

Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents. 



 

About Power Plus Systems: 



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission. 



 

Objectives: 

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.



 

Competencies: 

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.



 

Equipment Requirements:  

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.



 

Physical Requirements: 

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

 

Benefits: 

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply: 



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below. 




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply. 



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

title: SEO Link Builder (Outreach Specialist)
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and proactive SEO Link Builder who can consistently acquire high-quality backlinks using a range of proven link building strategies. This role requires someone who understands both free (organic outreach) and paid (placement-based) link acquisition methods and knows how to apply them strategically to drive rankings and organic growth.



Key Responsibilities:



Plan and execute scalable link building campaigns across multiple strategies

Build high-quality backlinks using a mix of free and paid methods, including outreach, partnerships, and placements

Consistently deliver up to 30 new quality backlinks per month per website, while maintaining strict quality standards

Manage and execute link building campaigns across multiple websites simultaneously

Prospect, qualify, and prioritise backlink opportunities based on SEO impact

Conduct personalised outreach to website owners, editors, and publishers

Negotiate placements where required while maintaining quality standards

Perform competitor backlink analysis to identify gaps and opportunities

Maintain outreach pipelines and manage relationships with publishers

Track, measure, and report on link performance (rankings, traffic impact, indexation)



Core Link Building Strategies You Must Be Proficient In:



Guest posting (manual outreach and placements)

Niche edits / link insertions

Broken link building

Moving Man Method

Resource page link building

Digital PR / HARO-style outreach

Competitor backlink replication



Backlink Qualification Criteria:

You must demonstrate a strong ability to evaluate link opportunities using the following metrics:



Relevance

Strong topical alignment with our niche

Contextual relevance of the linking page (not just the domain)

Authority

Domain Rating (DR) / Domain Authority (DA)

Referring domains profile

Organic keyword footprint (not just DR as a vanity metric)

Traffic Quality

Consistent organic traffic (via Ahrefs/SEMrush)

Keywords ranking in relevant niches

Avoid sites with inflated or irrelevant traffic sources

Outbound Link Profile

Ratio of outbound links to content

No spammy or irrelevant casino/adult/low-quality links

Editorial standards of the site

Indexation & Visibility

Pages are indexed in Google

Site is actively ranking and not deindexed or penalised

Recent content is being crawled and ranked



Required Skills and Experience:



Proven experience building high-quality backlinks that improve rankings

Demonstrated ability to consistently deliver up to 30 quality backlinks per month per website

Proven ability to manage link building across multiple websites at the same time

Strong understanding of both white-hat and grey-hat link building approaches

Ability to scale outreach while maintaining link quality

Experience with tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Hunter, Pitchbox, or similar

Strong written communication for outreach and negotiation

Ability to identify low-quality or risky links before acquisition



Application Requirement (Mandatory):

As part of your application, you must submit a 3–5 minute Loom video explaining the Moving Man Method.



Your video must clearly cover:



What the Moving Man Method is

How to find link opportunities (step-by-step)

Tools you would use (e.g., Ahrefs, Google search operators)

How you would structure your outreach message

Why this method works and when to use it



Applications without a Loom video will not be considered.

title: Social Media Outreach Specialist
description:
DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES



Research and identify relevant Customers, YouTubers and content creators that would fit our business’ industry

Present our products and propose collaborations, offering product samples for the content creator to review.

Ensure timely receipt of order confirmations and estimated time of arrival (ETA) screenshots from content creators.

Coordinate the sample distribution process, including verifying order placements to creators.

Facilitate the content creation by guiding them on recommended images/videos and also collecting Amazon review links from content creators.

title: Amazon Compliance Specialist
description:
Regulatory & Legal Compliance



Conduct in-depth research on FDA, EPA, FTC, and other relevant federal and state regulations.

Ensure compliance with international product safety standards (EU, UK, Canada, Asia-Pacific) when applicable.

Perform ongoing monitoring of regulatory updates and interpret implications for product categories.

Support intellectual property due diligence to prevent patent, copyright, or trademark infringement.



Risk Management & Product Compliance



Evaluate new and existing products for compliance risks and advise stakeholders on mitigation strategies.

Develop compliance testing protocols and coordinate with third-party labs and certification agencies.

Manage corrective actions, recalls, or withdrawals in alignment with Amazon’s policies and legal requirements.

Conduct audits of product listings and seller submissions to prevent unlawful or restricted product selection.



Cross-Functional Collaboration



Partner with Product Development, Supply Chain, and Seller Performance teams to execute compliance initiatives.

Provide clear guidance to vendors, suppliers, and internal teams on compliance requirements.

Train cross-functional stakeholders on compliance best practices and emerging regulatory risks.

Act as a subject matter expert to business teams during product launches, regulatory reviews, and inspections.



Policy & Process Development



Draft and implement compliance procedures, standard operating protocols (SOPs), and escalation processes.

Support automation and system-based compliance controls to scale monitoring and enforcement.

Contribute to internal policy development regarding restricted products, product claims, and safety certifications.

Continuously improve workflows to enhance accuracy, timeliness, and audit readiness.



Documentation & Reporting



Maintain accurate records of compliance assessments, approvals, and regulatory correspondence.

Prepare detailed reports and risk assessments for leadership review.

Provide supervisors with regular feedback on task progress, emerging risks, and resolution status.

Ensure confidentiality of sensitive data, supplier information, and Amazon business strategy.





Experience interacting directly with regulators including federal and state agencies

Experience in research and analyzing federal and state regulations

Familiarity and experience in dealing with FDA regulation is REQUIRED

Familiarity with international compliance frameworks (REACH, RoHS, CE marking).

Experience in compliance auditing, regulatory affairs, or legal review.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.

Excellent communication skills for engaging with global teams and regulators.

Ability to handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment with strict deadline

title: Youtube Video Editor
description:
Job Description:

We are looking for a talented and detail-oriented video editor to help create high-quality, engaging videos. The ideal candidate should have experience working with raw footage from phone recordings and be able to enhance content through expert editing, seamless transitions, and creative additions.



What You'll Do:

1. Tailored Workflow & Enhanced Quality: Optimize raw footage with noise reduction, audio leveling, and effects to elevate overall content quality.

2. Precise Editing & Efficiency: Edit and enhance videos while ensuring quick turnaround times without compromising quality.

3. Professional Video Editing: Deliver precise and creative edits to match the project’s needs.

4. Post-Production: Provide comprehensive post-production services to ensure polished, professional results.

5. Color Correction: Apply color grading and correction to enhance the visual appeal of videos.

6. Sound Design: Add background music, improve audio quality, and create an immersive audio experience.

7. Creative Storytelling: Make decisions on where to insert B-rolls, animations, zoom transitions, and graphics to make videos more engaging.

8. Subtitles & Captions: Add subtitles for accessibility and engagement.

9. Organizing Clips & Footage: Structure videos from beginning to end with a seamless flow.

10. Transitions & Video Effects: Implement smooth transitions and effects for a professional touch.

11. Intro & Outro: Create engaging intros and outros to enhance brand identity.

12. Multiple Revisions: Open to feedback and revisions to achieve the desired final product.



Ideal Candidate:



- Experienced in editing phone-recorded content

- Creative and detail-oriented

- Understands sound design and background music selection

- Proficient in color grading and correction

- Skilled in using professional video editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, Final Cut Pro, etc.)

- Efficient and deadline-driven



If you're passionate about video editing and can transform raw footage into engaging, high-quality content, we'd love to hear from you!



To apply, please send:

1. Examples of past video editing work

2. Are you willing to sign an NDA before getting the materials to edit?

3. Your typical turnaround time for a 3-5 minute video

4. Are you willing to create a short sample? We will provide raw footage for the test.

title: Short-Form Video Editor – Personal Brand (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Job Description:



I’m a 26-year-old entrepreneur based in Koh Samui, Thailand, running a portfolio of businesses. I’m building a personal brand on TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and YouTube long-form. I need a dedicated short-form video editor who can turn daily raw footage into 3 polished short-form videos per day (and eventually 1 long-form per week).



This is a full-time, long-term position. You will be my only editor. I film everything on iPhone and Insta360. Every evening I send you raw footage + a 60-second WhatsApp voice note briefing what each video should be. You deliver the 3 finished videos by the next morning (your time).



Style Reference:



The visual style I want is modeled after Trey Gustafson on 
 his content before applying. Key elements of this style:



Cinematic color grading – moody, dark tones with lifted shadows, desaturated midtones, teal/orange color split

Music-driven editing – cuts sync to the beat, pacing follows the energy of the track



Clean, minimal captions – bold sans-serif text, centered or lower-third, no flashy animations or emoji overlays

Smooth transitions – speed ramps, whip pans, match cuts between locations



Cinematic B-roll integration – tight product shots, environment shots, lifestyle footage layered over voiceover

Premium feel – this is NOT the typical fast-cut, 
-style TikTok editing. It’s elevated, clean, and intentional



Your Daily Workflow:



- I send you raw footage (iPhone 14 + Insta360 Go 3S clips) via Google Drive every evening

- I send a 60-second WhatsApp voice note explaining the 3 videos I want: the hook, the angle, and which clips to use

- You edit, color grade, add music, add captions, and deliver 3 finished short-form videos (30–90 seconds each)

- Turnaround: same day or by next morning (within 12 hours of receiving the brief)

- Deliver via Google Drive in 9:16 format, optimized for TikTok/Reels/Shorts



Required Skills:

- Proficient in CapCut Pro, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve (CapCut Pro preferred for speed)

- Strong color grading ability – you must be able to replicate the cinematic look described above, not just slap a filter on

- Typography and caption design – clean, readable, brand-consistent text overlays

- Music selection and beat-synced editing – you source the tracks (royalty-free) and cut to the rhythm

- Understanding of short-form content pacing – you know how to keep viewers past the first 3 seconds

- Reliable internet and able to download/upload large video files daily

- Available for WhatsApp communication during briefing hours (evening Thailand time / morning Philippines time)



Nice to Have (Not Required):

- Experience editing for entrepreneur/business content creators

- Familiarity with TikTok and Instagram Reels trends

- Ability to suggest hook improvements or creative angles based on what you’re seeing perform well

- Experience with YouTube long-form editing (will be added to your scope later)



Compensation:

$750–$1,000 USD/month (full-time), depending on skill level and experience. + Performance bonuses when content hits milestones. This rate will increase as the brand grows and your scope expands to include long-form editing.



How to Apply:

Send me:

Your portfolio or 3 examples of short-form videos you’ve edited (links to TikTok, Reels, or YouTube Shorts you edited)

A brief note on which editing software you use and your daily availability



Your honest assessment: look at Trey Gustafson’s TikTok 
 and tell me – can you replicate this style? What would you do differently?



If your application is strong, I’ll send you a paid test clip ($20) to edit before making a final decision.

title: Professional Dancer or Choreography Performer for AI Motion Capture Videos
description:
Job Overview



We are looking for a highly skilled dancer or movement performer who can quickly learn and accurately

replicate choreography for AI motion training purposes. This role focuses purely on movement

performance. The recorded videos will never be publicly posted — they are used internally for AI training

and motion reference only.

If you are someone who can study a dance, copy it precisely, and perform it confidently on camera, this

role is for you.



What You Will Do

• Study short choreography clips

• Accurately recreate movements, timing, and energy

• Record clean, high-quality videos of your performance

• Deliver videos according to guidelines

• Repeat and refine movements when needed

• Maintain consistency in posture, angles, and body control

All recordings are for internal use only and will not be published.



Requirements — Essential Skills

• Strong dancing ability

• Able to quickly memorize choreography

• Excellent body control and rhythm

• High attention to detail in movement replication

• Comfortable performing in front of a camera

• Able to follow structured instructions



Technical Requirements

• Modern smartphone with high-quality camera (iPhone 12 or newer, or equivalent Android)

• Ability to record in 1080p or 4K

• Stable internet connection for uploads

• Clean and neutral background available at home

• Good lighting setup or access to natural daylight



Preferred

• Experience in TikTok or Instagram dance trends

• Background in hip hop, commercial dance, K-Pop, or freestyle

• Previous performance or dance training

• Female performers preferred, but male performers are welcome if highly skilled

Ideal Personality

• Fast learner

• Reliable and punctual

• Open to feedback

• Professional attitude

• Comfortable repeating takes until perfect

• Good understanding of body positioning and camera awareness



Work Details

Location: Remote

Hours: Flexible

Type: Project-based or part-time

Compensation: Paid per video or monthly retainer

description:
