title: Backyard Design & Permit Research VA for Luxury Hillside Home Project – Claremont, CA
description:
We are looking for a highly capable, detail-obsessed Virtual Assistant to help lead the design coordination, research, and planning process for a luxury backyard project at our home in Claremont, California.



This is not a basic Pinterest or Canva role.



We want someone who can take a high-end inspiration concept and turn it into a serious, organized, buildable project package that helps us move toward real-world execution with the correct local professionals.



The backyard we want is inspired by elevated luxury hillside designs with features such as:

 • resort-style pool and spa

 • multi-level hardscape / terraced layout

 • integrated stairs and retaining features

 • fire feature

 • premium lighting

 • outdoor lounge / entertaining areas

 • strong visual symmetry

 • clean, modern, upscale finish selections

 • hillside-aware planning and drainage-conscious design



The property is on a sloped lot in Claremont, CA, so the design must be grounded in real feasibility, not just aesthetics.



Your Role



You will act as a design coordinator + research lead + project management VA.



You will help us:

 1. translate our inspiration into a realistic design direction

 2. research local permitting, zoning, pool, retaining wall, grading, drainage, and hillside constraints

 3. organize all findings into a clear decision-making package

 4. source and coordinate the right professionals needed for permit-ready plans

 5. help us avoid wasting time on concepts that are not actually buildable



Core Responsibilities

 • Review our inspiration images/videos and extract the key design language

 • Create a polished concept brief for the backyard vision

 • Develop 2–3 layout concepts based on the lot conditions and slope challenges

 • Research City of Claremont requirements related to:

 • pool/spa permits

 • retaining walls

 • grading

 • slope setbacks

 • fencing / pool barriers

 • drainage considerations

 • landscape documentation requirements

 • Call or
 local city departments, contractors, designers, engineers, and expeditors when needed

 • Build a list of required professionals for execution, such as:

 • pool designer / pool contractor

 • landscape architect or landscape designer

 • structural engineer

 • civil engineer

 • geotechnical engineer

 • surveyor

 • permit expeditor

 • Source highly rated local firms in Southern California / Inland Empire / Los Angeles County

 • Build vendor comparison sheets with:

 • scope

 • pricing

 • timing

 • reviews

 • strengths / weaknesses

 • Prepare a likely permitting roadmap and project sequence

 • Organize everything into clean folders, trackers, summaries, and presentations

 • Help coordinate revisions and next steps until we have a serious pre-construction direction



Deliverables



We expect the VA to produce:

 • a luxury concept / inspiration board

 • a backyard vision brief

 • a site feasibility research summary

 • city/permit research notes with sources

 • a list of likely required permits and consultant types

 • a local vendor shortlist

 • a rough project phasing plan

 • a budget range matrix

 • a decision memo on the best next step

 • weekly progress updates with real movement, not filler



Required Skills

 • Excellent research ability

 • Strong English writing and communication

 • Ability to think critically, not just gather surface-level information

 • Experience coordinating construction, architecture, landscape, pool, or permitting projects

 • Ability to create polished summaries, spreadsheets, and presentations

 • Strong visual taste for luxury residential design

 • Organized, responsive, and proactive

 • Able to distinguish between a concept drawing and something actually permit-feasible



Strong Preference For Candidates With Experience In:

 • luxury residential projects

 • pool / spa design coordination

 • hillside or slope-site projects

 • California permitting research

 • landscape architecture support

 • construction coordination

 • vendor sourcing and bid comparison



Important



This role is about building a real-world actionable plan.



We are not looking for someone who only sends inspiration photos or generic design ideas.



We want someone who can help bridge the gap between:

vision -> feasibility -> professional team -> execution



To Apply



Please send:

 1. a short introduction

 2. examples of any design coordination, architecture, pool, landscape, or permitting-related work

 3. examples of research summaries / project trackers / presentations you have created

 4. a brief explanation of how you would approach a luxury hillside backyard project in California

 5. your hourly rate

 6. your weekly availability



Bonus



In your application, include the phrase:

“permit-first design wins”



This helps us know you actually read the full post.

title: Executive Virtual Assistant
description:

About Us:



Simplify AI is revolutionizing healthcare delivery by providing AI-powered solutions designed specifically for clinics. We're scaling rapidly across US, Singapore, and Australia, and we need a force multiplier—someone who can anticipate needs, execute independently, and keep our operations running smoothly.



The Role:



You're not a typical VA. You're a trusted operator who thinks like a founder. You'll manage calendars,
s, projects, and communications—but more importantly, you'll identify bottlenecks, solve problems before they arise, and drive initiatives that move the needle. You'll be the backbone of our operations, handling everything from scheduling to strategic coordination.



What You'll Do:



Core Responsibilities:



Manage founder/leadership calendar across multiple time zones (US, Singapore, Australia)

Filter, prioritize, and respond to
s on behalf of leadership (with authority to make decisions)

Schedule and coordinate meetings, pitches, and calls with clinic prospects and partners

etings and decisions

Manage project timelines and deadlines across teams

Research and implement tools that improve productivity

Coordinate hiring, onboarding, and team logistics

Monitor market conditions, competitor activity, and trends relevant to our business

Proactively flag risks, opportunities, and strategic decisions that need attention

What We're Looking For:



Non-Negotiable:



Exceptional English Communication: Written and verbal fluency. You're drafting
s on behalf of leadership, so your communication must be polished, professional, and persuasive.

Independent Problem-Solver: You don't wait for instructions. You see a problem and fix it. You ask questions only when necessary and propose solutions.

Strategic Thinking: You understand business priorities. You know what matters and what doesn't. You can prioritize ruthlessly.

Initiative-Taker: You're constantly looking for ways to improve operations, streamline processes, and create leverage. You're uncomfortable with inefficiency.

Reliability & Ownership: You own your deliverables. You follow through. You're organized and detail-oriented without being micro-managed.

Adaptability: You work across multiple time zones, contexts, and priorities. You're comfortable with ambiguity and changing priorities in a startup.

Discretion & Professionalism: You handle sensitive information confidently. You're a trusted advisor, not just a task manager.

Preferred Experience:



3+ years as an executive assistant, operations manager, or similar high-autonomy role

Experience in startups, scale-ups, or fast-paced environments

Familiarity with CRM tools (HubSpot, Pipedrive, etc.)

Project management experience (Asana, 
, Notion, etc.)

Sales operations or business development background

Compensation & Benefits:

Flexible remote work arrangement

Professional development budget

Flexible hours (core hours required, but flexibility on timing)

What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days):



Month 1: You've mastered the calendar,
 flow, and project tracking. Leadership feels relief.

Month 2: You've identified 3-5 operational inefficiencies and implemented fixes. You're proactive, not reactive.

Month 3: You're running initiatives independently. You've built systems that scale. Leadership trusts you completely.

Why This Role Matters:



Founders and leaders are only as effective as their operations. A great VA multiplies a leader's capacity by 3-5x. If you can think strategically, act independently, and care about outcomes (not just tasks), this role will make you invaluable to our team and our mission.



How to Apply:



Send an
 to 
 with subject header: I AM A ROCKSTAR VA



Application Requirements:

Cover Letter: (500 words max) Answer this: "What's one operational inefficiency you've identified and solved in a previous role, and how did you approach it?"

Writing Sample: Provide 2-3 professional
s you've written on behalf of leadership (or similar high-stakes communication)

Video Introduction: (2 minutes) Introduce yourself, highlight why you're excited about this role, and give us a sense of your communication style and personality

title: Virtual Assistant (Xero + Construction Admin) – Part Time | Long Term
description:
Virtual Assistant – Construction (Xero, Wunderbuild + Admin Support)

Part-Time | Remote | Approx. 5 hours per week (with growth potential)

About Us

AMG Carpentry & Construction is an Australian residential building company specialising in high-quality renovations, extensions, and custom builds.

We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to support our accounting administration (Xero) and project systems (Wunderbuild).

This is a long-term opportunity with the potential to increase hours and responsibility as the business grows.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities

Xero & Accounting Support

• Reconcile bank transactions in Xero

• Upload and correctly allocate supplier invoices

• Match invoices to suppliers and relevant projects

• Cross-check subcontractor invoices against timesheets

Project & Systems Support

• Assist with setup and ongoing organisation of Wunderbuild

• Maintain and organise project files and documentation

Construction Administration

• Assist with preparation and formatting of building contracts

• Assist with subcontractor agreements

• General administrative support across projects

General VA Tasks

•
 and inbox management

• File management (Google Drive / Dropbox)

• Data entry and document formatting

• Ad-hoc administrative tasks as required

________________________________________

Requirements

Essential:

• Proven experience using Xero

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy

• Excellent organisational skills

• Strong written English communication

• Ability to work independently and manage time effectively

• Reliable internet connection

Highly Regarded:

• Experience working with construction or building companies

• Familiarity with project management software (Wunderbuild or similar)

• Experience with invoice processing and purchase orders

________________________________________

Why Join Us?

• Flexible, remote work

• Supportive family run business and a growing team

• Opportunity for increased hours and long-term role

________________________________________

How to Apply

To help us identify detail-oriented applicants, please include the word “Builder” in your application and answer the following:

1. What experience do you have using Xero? (briefly outline tasks you’ve handled)

Applications that do not follow these instructions may not be considered.

title: Customer Service Representative (Airbnb Guest Support – Remote)
description:
Nomad Equity is a growing U.S.-based short-term rental management company that manages vacation rental properties across the United States.



Our mission is to deliver an exceptional guest experience while operating high-quality properties for our homeowners.



We are looking for a reliable and professional Guest Communications Specialist to join our remote team and help support our Airbnb and VRBO guests.



This role is extremely important because you will be the primary point of contact for guests staying in our homes.



What You’ll Be Responsible For



• Responding to guest messages quickly and professionally

• Answering questions before arrival and providing check-in instructions

• Helping guests during their stay and resolving issues calmly

• Coordinating maintenance issues with our operations team

• Escalating urgent problems when needed

• Responding to guest reviews when necessary

• Ensuring every guest interaction delivers a 5-star hospitality experience



Our goal is simple: every guest should feel taken care of and supported.



Work Schedule



You must be available during U.S. Pacific Time (California).



Initial Schedule:

Monday – Friday

9:00 AM – 6:00 PM PST



As our team grows, this role may transition into evening or overnight shifts, so schedule flexibility is important.



Requirements



• Excellent written & verbal English (very important)

• Strong customer service and problem-solving skills

• Ability to stay calm and professional in stressful situations

• Ability to respond quickly and handle multiple conversations at once

• Reliable high-speed internet connection

• Quiet work environment

• Experience working with Airbnb, VRBO, or short-term rental companies

• Experience using guest messaging platforms (Hostaway, Guesty, Zendesk, etc.)





The Ideal Candidate



• Hospitality-minded and naturally helpful

• Extremely responsive and organized

• Detail-oriented and dependable

• Comfortable handling urgent guest situations

• Takes ownership and communicates clearly



Why Join Us



• Work with a professional U.S. company

• Long-term stable position

• Fully remote work environment

• Clear systems and support from our operations team

• Opportunity to grow as the company expands



How to Apply



To move forward, please complete our quick application form — it only takes a few minutes: ????

- Make outbound calls and
s to construction businesses

title: Operations Manager - Short-Term Rental Portfolio (Airbnb)
description:
Nomad Equity is a growing short-term rental property management company looking for an experienced Operations Manager to take full ownership of day-to-day property operations across our portfolio.



This is not a guest communications role. We need someone who has been in the trenches of STR operations — coordinating maintenance, managing cleaning teams, running inspections, handling Airbnb resolution cases, and producing accurate owner financials. If you've done this work before, you know exactly what this role looks like.



What You'll Own:

- Maintenance issues and quarterly property inspections across all properties

- Cleaning team management, including sending the cleaning coordinator the next day's check-in and check-out schedule daily

- Purchasing consumable supplies for all properties

- Bad review disputes — any review 4 stars or below gets added to 
 and you work with the guest and Airbnb to get it removed

- Airbnb resolution cases end-to-end

- Categorizing and identifying transactions throughout the month

- Monthly owner statements, drafting owner performance
s, and transferring owner funds after admin approval

- Enforcing sub-5-minute response time to all guest messages and inquiries

- Processing guest refunds

- Ensuring the task queue is being cleared on a daily basis



What We're Looking For:

- Significant hands-on experience managing short-term rental operations — not just guest messaging, but actual property ops including maintenance coordination, vendor management, cleaning logistics, inspections, and supply chain

- Experience with financial reporting in the STR space: owner statements, transaction categorization, and fund transfers

- Familiarity with Airbnb's resolution center and review dispute process

- Experience with project management tools like 
 or Asana for tracking operational tasks

- Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal

- Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and enforce standards across a team

- Strong problem-solving skills for real-world Airbnb operational situations



Schedule: Wednesday–Sunday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Pacific Standard Time

Compensation: Hourly (rate based on experience)



How to Apply:

Please apply using this Google Form link: 



After submitting the form, you'll be invited to a brief AI-powered voice interview. This helps us quickly assess communication skills and operational experience so we can move qualified candidates forward faster. We look forward to hearing from you.

description:

Experience Required

? Strong time management skills and the ability to handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.

How We Will Help You Succeed

title: Brand & Logo Designer Wanted — Ongoing Contractor
description:
BRAND & LOGO DESIGNER WANTED - ONGOING CONTRACTOR ROLE



WQ Creative is an Australian brand and website design studio. We work with businesses to build strategic, cohesive brands and we're looking for a talented graphic designer based in the Philippines to join us as an ongoing contractor to take on logo and brand identity work.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



You'll be the designer behind brand identity packages for our clients - logos, submarks, colour systems, typography selections, brand guidelines, and supporting collateral. Work comes in regularly, and we need someone who can hit the brief and deliver polished, professional results.





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



• A designer who lives and breathes brand identity - not just pretty logos, but thoughtful, strategic brand systems

• Strong understanding of brand hierarchy, visual language, and how a brand works across touchpoints

• Comfortable working to a creative brief and collaborating with a strategist

• Reliable turnaround times and clean file delivery

• Professional with client-quality output, every time





NON-NEGOTIABLES



• You must work in Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign, or Affinity Designer/Publisher - no Canva, no shortcuts

• A portfolio of real brand identity work is required to be considered - logos alone aren't enough, we want to see full brand systems

• You must be able to receive a brief and work with minimal hand-holding

• No AI-generated logos. We're a design studio and our work needs to be craft-led





THE ROLE



• Type: Ongoing contractor / freelance

• Volume: Project-based, regular flow of work

• Location: Philippines-based applicants only

• Communication: English required





TO APPLY



Send us:



• A link to your portfolio (Behance, personal site, or PDF - no Instagram grids please)

• A brief note on your background and design approach

• Your rates (per project or hourly - whichever you prefer)



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.



We're not just hiring a pair of hands. We're looking for a designer who takes brand seriously. If that's you, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Auction Listing Sourcer
description:
We are hiring someone to browse online auction listings and flag items that match our buying criteria. You will scroll through large volumes of listings across auction platforms and send us anything you think fits what we're looking for. Over time, we will give you yes or no feedback on each listing you send so you can develop a strong understanding of our preferences and what we are looking for. This role requires excellent attention to detail, patience with repetitive browsing tasks, and the ability to learn quickly for listings we would want to purchase.



Responsibilities:

Browse auction listing platforms for several hours per day. Identify listings that you believe match our buying criteria and send them to us. You will be responsible for reviewing hundreds of pages of listings per session and sending us a consistent stream of candidate listings throughout your shift. Over time you will receive yes or no feedback on each listing you send, and you are expected to adapt quickly to our preferences as they evolve. 



Requirements:

Strong English comprehension is required so you can accurately read and interpret listing descriptions. You must have excellent attention to detail and the ability to stay focused while browsing large volumes of repetitive content for extended periods. You must be comfortable with a high-volume, detail-focused workflow and take accuracy seriously. A reliable internet connection and the ability to work independently without supervision are required.



Ideal Candidate:

The ideal candidate is patient, consistent, and genuinely good at spotting things others might overlook based on previous listings we have approved from you. You should be comfortable doing focused, repetitive work for long sessions and take pride in sending quality leads rather than just quantity. You must be coachable and willing to adjust based on feedback.



How to Apply:

Please include a short explanation of why you think you would be a good fit for this role, and describe any experience you have browsing, buying, or selling on online auction or resale platforms and any past experience with video games or gaming hardware. Applications without this information may not be considered.

title: CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE COORDINATOR (FAMILY EXPERIENCE TEAM | ATTIO PREFERRED)
description:
ABOUT RELATIONAL MINDS



Relational Minds is an Australian child and family mental health organisation delivering relationship-focused, evidence-informed programs to parents, children, educators, and leaders. Our philosophy is grounded in relationship-first approaches, compassionate communication, and trauma-informed, evidence-based practice.



THE OPERATIONAL CONTEXT



We coordinate complex assessment and treatment pathways involving multiple clinicians and strict timelines. Our Family Experience Team ensures every family’s journey is organised, visible, and followed through.



We are transitioning from spreadsheet-based tracking to a modern relationship management system using Attio CRM. Attio will serve as our central system for managing family journeys, lifecycle stages, and service coordination.



WHY WORK WITH US

	•	Values: We value kindness, clarity, and reliable teamwork.

	•	Culture: We offer a calm, respectful, emotionally intelligent work environment.

	•	Leadership: You will work with supportive Australian leadership that values initiative and good judgement.

	•	Growth: This is a meaningful long-term role where you will help shape how our systems evolve as the organisation grows.



THE ROLE



You will coordinate the end-to-end journey for families, acting as the operational guide who ensures their progress through our service stays organised and on schedule.



This is a coordination role requiring assertive communication, structured thinking, and strong system management.



You will work primarily within Attio CRM, helping maintain a clear view of where each family sits in their journey, ensuring tasks are completed on time, and identifying risks before delays occur.



You will work within a small Customer Experience team and report to the Customer Experience Manager.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

	•	Manage the family journey

Track each family’s progress from intake through assessment, treatment blocks, and follow-up using Attio pipelines and lifecycle stages.

	•	Maintain data integrity

Ensure Attio remains the central “source of truth” for family records, service stages, and communication history.

	•	Coordinate timelines

Monitor clinician and administrative tasks and follow up when deadlines are approaching or overdue.

	•	System management

Maintain clean, structured CRM records including custom fields, tags, and views that allow the team to quickly see where attention is needed.

	•	Risk identification

Identify delays, missing information, or stalled cases and escalate to the Customer Experience Manager.

	•	Documentation coordination

Ensure consents, reports, and key documents are correctly linked and stored within our Microsoft 365 environment.



REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

	•	Strong CRM experience

Experience using a CRM to manage customer or client journeys across multiple stages.

	•	Attio CRM experience strongly preferred

Familiarity with Attio pipelines, custom fields, views, and workflow organisation.

	•	Structured data management

Experience maintaining accurate records and ensuring systems remain clean and reliable.

	•	Workflow coordination

Proven ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders and follow up professionals to ensure timelines are met.

	•	Professional written communication

Strong written English and the ability to communicate calmly with Australian families.

	•	Experience working with Australian companies

Understanding Australian professional expectations and communication style.



HIGHLY REGARDED

	•	Experience in Australian healthcare, allied health, or NDIS-related services

	•	Experience implementing or managing CRM systems during organisational growth

	•	Familiarity with automation tools such as Zapier, Power Automate, or CRM workflow automation

	•	Background in operations coordination, client success, or service delivery



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

	•	Not a general Virtual Assistant role

	•	Not a passive data-entry position

	•	Not a call centre role



You will actively coordinate workflows and ensure families and clinicians stay on track.



WORK DETAILS

	•	Remote position (work from home)

	•	Full-time, long-term role

	•	Work aligned with Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) business hours

	•	Direct collaboration with an Australian leadership team



HOW TO APPLY



Please include in your application:

	•	A summary of your experience using CRM systems to track customer or client journeys

	•	Your experience using Attio CRM or similar platforms

	•	Your experience working with Australian companies

	•	One example of how you followed up with professionals to keep a workflow on schedule



Shortlisted candidates will complete a short practical task demonstrating how they would structure and manage a client journey within a CRM system.

title: Facebook & Google Ads Manager
description:
**About RM Tires:**



RM Tires is a growing U.S.-based tire shop focused on scaling customer acquisition through digital marketing. Our goal is to build a consistent, predictable, and scalable advertising system that generates leads, phone calls, and in-store sales.



We are not looking for someone to “run ads.” We are looking for someone who can drive results.



**Job Overview:**



We are seeking a performance-driven Ads Manager who can plan, execute, and optimize campaigns across
 and Google. This role requires someone who understands how to turn ad spend into measurable results.



You will be responsible for managing campaigns end-to-end from strategy and setup to optimization and reporting. You should be comfortable analyzing data, identifying problems, and making decisions that improve performance.



**Key Responsibilities:**

- Plan, launch, and manage
 & Instagram ad campaigns

- Manage Google Ads campaigns (Search, Performance Max, and Local campaigns)

- Monitor and optimize campaigns to improve performance and return on ad spend (ROAS)

- Conduct A/B testing on creatives, audiences, and messaging

- Set up and manage tracking (Meta Pixel, conversion tracking, etc.)

- Analyze campaign data and provide clear reports and actionable insights

- Collaborate with the team on ad creatives and overall strategy

- Continuously test, learn, and scale what works



**Qualifications:**

- Proven experience managing
 and Google Ads campaigns

- Strong understanding of key metrics (ROAS, CPA, CTR, conversions)

- Experience optimizing and scaling campaigns

- Ability to analyze data and make performance-driven decisions

- Reliable communication and ability to meet deadlines



**Preferred Qualifications:**

- Experience working with local businesses or automotive-related industries

- Familiarity with lead generation campaigns (calls, messages, form submissions)

- Basic understanding of landing pages and conversion optimization

- Ability to suggest improvements for ad creatives and messaging



**To Apply for the Job:**



Please follow the instructions exactly. Applications that do not follow directions will not be considered.



1. Change the subject line to:

I Want to Work for You [(3321)^.5+COS(2411)+48283*34/(31231^1.1)= ] - VA" NOTE*** You can round the number to the nearest 10th

2. At the top of your message, write a maximum of 3 sentences explaining why you want this position and why you are a good fit.

3. Do not write more than 3 sentences or your application will be deleted.

4. Provide links to 2–3 ad campaigns you have managed (screenshots or case studies showing results are preferred).

5. Include your
 or Google Ads experience summary (accounts handled, budgets managed, industries worked with).

6. Explain your process for launching a new ad campaign from start to finish.

7. Describe a campaign you worked on that did well. What made it successful?

8. Which platform are you stronger in
 or Google? Explain why.

9. Share any additional experience that would make you a strong fit for this role.



Make sure to follow all steps. This is a long-term opportunity with room for bonuses and growth, so we are only looking for serious applicants who understand performance marketing.



If you have any questions, feel free to ask.



Thanks,

Ryan Mastrojohn

s — screen, prioritise, and draft replies

 work

 with the subject line: “Personal Executive Assistant” and include clear answers to ALL of the following:

title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	-	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	-	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	-	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	-	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	-	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	-	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	-	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	-	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	-	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	-	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	-	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	-	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	-	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	-	Meet deadlines without being chased

	-	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	-	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	-	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	-	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	-	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	-	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	-	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	-	Own laptop suitable for business use

	-	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: Quality Assurance Specialist for Media VA
description:
DEPARTMENT: Project Coordination

REPORTS TO: CEO

WORK HOURS, PART TIME: 9AM-12PM, 8PM-10PM Mountain Time or similar

STARTING SALARY: $5 per hour

POTENTIAL SALARY: $600-$800 / Month



SUMMARY

We’re looking for someone smart, organized, and excited to grow with us! If you’re a positive, detail-oriented person who loves to learn, takes feedback well, and wants to be part of a close-knit, supportive team, we’d love to meet you.

This is a long-term role for someone who’s ready to take on more responsibility over time and become a key part of our journey.



Who We Are:

Chelwood Media is a woman-run real estate media company based in Utah. We create amazing photo and video content for realtors, and we’re known for our fast turnaround, friendly service, and high-quality work. Our team is growing quickly, and we’re passionate about doing great work and loving the team we work with.

If you’re looking for a place where your ideas matter, you’ll be supported, and you can grow, this might be the perfect fit.



Job Summary

Manage, review for quality, upload, and retouch photos and video on a daily basis. Follow up with the editing team on project status. Maintain communication with the admin team.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Project Management

Send out projects before 12 PM Mountain Time

Quality Control for Deliverables (Photos, Videos, 3D Tours)

Make edits to photos in photoshop/lightroom as needed

Make edits to Videos as needed

Communicate with Admin team on deliveries



SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

Ability to take on several tasks at once.

EXTREME attention to detail.

Excellent English speaking and written.

Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software experience.

Proficient in Canva, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Lightroom, Slack.

Preferred proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro/Final Cut Pro/Capcut.

Fast learner and willing to learn



INSTRUCTION TO APPLY

Please
 
 your resume, internet speed, and a video recording of you answering the following four questions.  Please make sure you send a video made specifically for this job post or you will not be considered.

1. Please tell us about yourself, what do you love?

2. Please detail your past work experience and how it relates to this job at Chelwood Media. 

3. Why do you believe you would be a great candidate for this position?

4. Do you have leadership or management experience?

title: Ad Creative Strategist for Multi-Brand Portfolio
description:
We are looking for a versatile Ad Creative Strategist to drive performance marketing for a portfolio of diverse ventures. We need someone who can switch gears effectively between high-end real estate, local wellness businesses, and D2C e-commerce.



The Industries

You will be creating strategies and assets for 8 different business lines across these categories:



- Wellness & Fitness: Pilates Studio, Yoga (Beachfront/Hotel), Teacher Training, and Workshops.

- Real Estate: Off-plan Development Sales and Luxury Villa Rentals/Brokerage.

- Travel: Wellness Retreats.

- B2B / B2C Ecom



The Engagement Model

- Phase 1 (Paid Trial): We will start with a fixed-price trial where you deliver one ad creative strategy + asset for each business line (or a selected mix) to test your ability to adapt your style.

- Phase 2 (Retainer): If the creatives perform and the workflow is smooth, we will move to a monthly retainer for ongoing testing and scaling.



What We Need

- Proven experience creating high-CTR ad creatives for Meta (FB/IG) and TikTok.

- Ability to adapt visual style and copy tone (e.g., Luxury/Professional for Real Estate vs. Aesthetic/Vibe for Pilates).

- Strong understanding of direct response marketing and hooks.



How to Apply

1. Share a link to your portfolio showing examples in Wellness, Real Estate, or Beauty/Ecom.

2. Quote your fixed price for the "Trial Phase" (based on creating 1 ad per business), and also what is your monthly retainer after successfully passed the trial.

3. Start your cover letter with the word "STRATEGY".

We are a fast-moving US-based entrepreneur running multiple AI-powered service businesses. Our tech stack is modern, our team is lean, and we move fast. We need a sharp tech VA who can jump in, get things done, and grow with us long term.

v — we are building client-facing platforms and portals on this tool and we need someone who knows it or can master it fast. Beyond that you will work across our full stack:

 — automation workflows connecting all our tools

Google Tag Manager and
 Pixel — tracking and conversion setup

Zapier — workflow automation

General tech support across the team as needed





YOU ARE THE RIGHT PERSON IF:

You learn new tools fast — we will throw something at you that you have not seen before and you will figure it out

v before

 scenarios with 10 or more steps

Real work on real businesses that are live and generating revenue

A team that communicates well, pays on time, and respects your time





HOW TO APPLY

We receive a lot of applications. Here is how to stand out.

v specifically — even if limited, tell us what you know

Your available hours and time zone

Your
 or Zoom ID for a short video interview

We look forward to meeting you.

title: Designer (Figma) — Remote Role, Australian Company, Philippines Hours
description:
We're Exit Forward Holdings,  a Perth-based equity growth firm that partners with founder-led businesses. We own and operate Dilate Digital (one of WA's leading digital marketing agencies), Online Creative Dudes, and a growing portfolio of businesses across Australia.



We're looking for a skilled brand designer based in the Philippines to join our team part-time, working Australian hours (9am–5pm AWST / Perth time). This role has a clear path to full-time for the right person as our portfolio grows.



You'll work directly with the two founders on design work across our brands,  capabilities documents, presentations, brand assets, social content, and digital collateral. We have a strong brand identity and clear guidelines. What we need is someone who can execute inside that system with precision, creativity, and minimal hand-holding.



WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON:



— High-quality PDF documents and capabilities decks designed in Figma

— Brand assets across Exit Forward

— Presentations and pitch materials for founder meetings

— Social media graphics and digital content

— Website graphics and landing page assets



YOU ARE THE RIGHT PERSON IF YOU:



— Have 3+ years of professional design experience

— Are highly proficient in Figma — this is non-negotiable

— Have a strong eye for typography, layout, and white space

— Can work within a strict brand system (black and white palette, specific typefaces) without losing creativity

— Communicate clearly and confidently in written and spoken English

— Are proactive — you ask good questions early, not after you've gone the wrong direction

— Are available and online during Perth business hours (9am–5pm AWST)

— Take feedback professionally and iterate quickly

— Have a portfolio that demonstrates clean, professional brand design work



BONUS POINTS:

— Experience designing capabilities decks or investor/sales documents

— Video Editing Experience 

— Experience working with Australian or international clients

— Designer / Developer 



WHAT WE OFFER:



— Stable part-time role with a clear path to full-time as the business grows

— Direct access to two experienced founders — you won't be lost in a big team

— Work on premium brand projects — not template spam

— Clear creative briefs with room to bring your eye to the work

— A team that respects your time and communicates well

— Salary reviewed based on performance, with full-time on the table for the right person



TO APPLY:



Please send your portfolio link and a brief note (in your own words — not a template) telling us why this role suits you and one project from your portfolio you're most proud of and why.



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

title: Executive Virtual Assistant | BTEC
description:
Company Description

We are a rapidly growing e-commerce company that sells a variety of products through platforms such as Meta ads, Google ads, Instagram, and more.



Role Description

We are seeking a highly organized, remote, contract Virtual Assistant to support our fast-paced e-commerce operations. In this role, you will be the backbone of our daily activities, handling tasks such as customer inquiries, exchanges and returns, basic administrative support, and coordinating with our marketing teams. Your primary responsibility will be ensuring smooth day-to-day operations to maximize efficiency and customer satisfaction. The ideal candidate thrives in a high-energy, dynamic environment, possesses excellent problem-solving skills, and has a strong understanding of modern e-commerce workflows.



Qualifications

At least 1 year of experience as a Virtual Assistant, particularly within the e-commerce sector.



Proficient in English for clear, effective communication with the team and our customers (at least C1 level).



Availability to work according to Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT).



Strong proficiency in standard office and administrative tools (e.g., Google Workspace, Microsoft Office).



Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, and maintain strict attention to detail.



Demonstrated experience in handling customer support inquiries and ensuring a positive, brand-aligned customer experience.



Qualities we seek: Adaptability, quick responsiveness, a strong eagerness to learn, and the ambition to progress to more significant roles in the future.



Location: This is a strictly REMOTE position.



To apply, please click on the "Apply" button on the listing and follow the instructions to submit your resume in English. Ensure your resume highlights your relevant experience to clearly demonstrate how your skills meet the specific requirements outlined in the job description.



Note: Experience with e-commerce platforms (such as Shopify) and customer helpdesk software (such as Zendesk or Gorgias) is a strong plus.

title: Referral Network Builder & Lead Research Specialist
description:
About Monetas

Monetas (
) is an Australian mortgage broking startup that helps homeowners find their lowest cost home loan by comparing 80+ lenders, including those that don't pay broker commissions. We return trail commission as ongoing cashback and re-optimise loans quarterly. We're looking for a detail-oriented, AI-savvy virtual assistant to help build our referral partner network from the ground up.



Role Summary

You will be responsible for building a database of potential referral partners (accountants, financial planners, conveyancers, and real estate agents) across target Sydney suburbs. The workflow is AI-assisted: you will use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini or similar) to generate initial prospect lists, then manually verify, enrich, and quality-check every contact before it enters our outreach pipeline. You will also support outreach logistics, track communication, and handle post-meeting admin.

This is not a role where you research everything from scratch. It's a role where you use AI to work fast and your own judgment to ensure everything is accurate. We value speed and accuracy in equal measure.



Hours: 15–20 hours/week (flexible schedule, AEST-aligned availability preferred)

Duration: Ongoing, starting with a 1-week trial period

Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)

Reports to: Founder (Rahul)



Key Responsibilities

1. AI-Assisted Research & List Building (Primary Focus)

* Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, etc) to generate initial lists of accountants, financial planners, buyers agents, mortgage-adjacent professionals and key contacts at employee rewards program administrators in target Sydney suburbs (North Sydney, Neutral Bay, Mosman, Cremorne, Chatswood, Lane Cove, and surrounding Lower North Shore areas)

* Manually verify every AI-generated contact: confirm the person exists, still works at the firm, and the
/phone details are correct by cross-referencing firm websites, LinkedIn, and business directories

* Enrich each contact with details AI can't reliably provide: whether they serve property investors, whether they already have a broker referral relationship, and a personalisation note based on their website or LinkedIn activity

* Flag and discard any AI-generated contacts that can't be verified (accuracy matters more than volume)

* Maintain and update a structured Google Sheets tracker with all contacts, verification status, personalisation notes, and outreach status

* Target: 25–30 verified, enriched contacts per week (AI generates the initial list of 50–80; you verify and enrich the best half)



2. Outreach Support

* Send pre-approved outreach
s individually from the founder's
 account (not bulk sends)

* Use AI to draft personalised first lines for each
 based on research notes, then refine for natural tone (subject to founder approval)

* Track
 opens, replies, and non-responses in the outreach spreadsheet

* Flag when follow-up
s 
 2, 3, or 4) are due based on the outreach sequence timing

* Schedule coffee meetings and calls in the founder's calendar



3. Post-Meeting Admin

* Send pre-drafted thank-you
s after the founder's meetings with referral partners

* Update the tracker with meeting notes and next steps

* Set follow-up reminders at appropriate intervals (typically 2 weeks post-meeting)

* Prepare and send one-pager or intro
 templates that referral partners can forward to their clients



Requirements

Must Have:

* Proficiency with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, or similar) for research tasks. You should already be using AI regularly in your work, not learning it for the first time

* Strong verification and critical thinking skills. AI will give you plausible but sometimes wrong information. Your job is to catch the errors before they reach our outreach pipeline

* Excellent written English (you will be drafting personalisation notes and handling
 logistics)

* Experience with Google Sheets (comfortable with filters, data validation, and basic formulas)

* High attention to detail and consistency in data entry

* Reliable internet connection and ability to work independently with minimal supervision

* Responsive communication during AEST business hours (at least 4–5 hours of overlap)



Nice to Have:

* Prior experience in lead generation, real estate admin, or financial services support

* Familiarity with Australian business directories, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, or similar prospecting tools

* Understanding of the Australian mortgage or financial services industry

* Experience building or refining AI prompts for research workflows



How to Apply

Please submit:

title: 7 Figure Business Ad Creative Editor (Needed for Script Execution)
description:
We run a high-volume direct response operation and need an editor who genuinely understands how DR ads work — not just someone who makes things look good, but someone who edits with the sell in mind.

This is a long-term role. If you're the right fit, you'll have consistent weekly work and room to grow with us.



What You'll Be Doing

Editing raw footage into high-converting ad creatives. You will receive scripts — no B-roll guidance, no asset folders, no hand-holding. Sourcing the right supporting content is entirely on you. This is one of the most important parts of the job and how we separate good editors from great ones. Your edits need to serve the argument, not just fill the screen.

As you grow into the role, you'll also be expected to learn and execute AI-generated talking head creatives — we'll walk you through the tools and process, but you need to come in curious and coachable.



Who We're Looking For

You've edited paid ad creatives before — not just YouTube or social content, but ads built to convert

You understand why B-roll choices matter in DR — you're reinforcing belief, not decorating

You can explain why an edit works, not just execute it

You're resourceful with content sourcing — stock footage, B-roll libraries, sound design, you know where to find what you need without being pointed to it

You're open to learning new workflows, specifically AI talking head creative production

You're fast, reliable, and don't need to be chased for deliverables

You take feedback well and bring ideas to the table

You're honest about your skill level and won't waste either of our time



How This Works — Two Stages

We don't throw trial tasks at everyone who applies. We filter first, and we only move forward with candidates who actually demonstrate they think in direct response terms. If you make it through the filter, the trial is paid.

Here's exactly how it goes:



Stage 1 — The Filter

This is how we find out if you actually understand DR before we go any further.

Step 1 — Secret word: Start your application with the word "CRISP" — if it's not there, your application won't be opened. This isn't a trick, it's a reading test.

Step 2 — One specific clip: Send us a single ad you edited that drove real results — ideally something with strong CTR or measurable engagement. We're not looking for a full portfolio reel. One ad, real numbers if you have them.

Step 3 — 60-second Loom: Record a short video — 60 seconds max. Don't tell us about yourself. Don't pitch us on why you want the role. Instead, pull up that ad you submitted and walk us through one specific cut or B-roll choice you made. Tell us why you made it and what it was supposed to do for the viewer. If you can't explain the psychology behind your own edit, we already have our answer.

Candidates who pass Stage 1 will be contacted directly to move to Stage 2.



Stage 2 — The Paid Trial

If we like what we see, we'll invite you to complete two trial tasks. Both are paid. This is where we see how you think when handed a real DR script with no creative direction — because that's exactly how this job runs.

Details will be shared directly with candidates who make it through.



Compensation

$60 per creative batch

$100–$200 bonus per winning ad — when one of your edits converts, you get paid for it

This isn't a flat hourly grind. As your edits start performing, your earnings grow with them.

Start: Immediate

title: Shopify Web Developer / eCommerce Feed Specialist (GMC + Local Inventory)
description:
We’re looking for an experienced Shopify developer / eCommerce feed specialist to help optimise our Shopify store, product feeds, and Google Merchant Center (GMC) integration.



This role is focused on resolving issues impacting local inventory accuracy, product feed structure, and Google integrations, and setting up a scalable, reliable system going forward.



Key Responsibilities



Shopify Setup & Data Structure



Shopify POS Lite setup and configuration

Product metafields and custom tagging (In-Store vs Online Only)

Product feed structure and attribute mapping

Shopify Admin API or bulk product editing for large-scale updates



Feed Management & Google Integration



Supplemental feed creation via Google Sheets

Troubleshooting Shopify ? Feed App ? GMC data flow

Feed app evaluation and migration (e.g. DataFeedWatch, Multifeeds, or similar)

Variant-level feed management (1,500+ variants with correct availability and store data)



Local Inventory & Store Integration (Priority)



Local inventory feed configuration:

Store codes

Pickup eligibility

Stock quantity mapping

Google Business Profile linkage with local inventory



App & Integration Management



Shopify app setup, replacement, and optimisation (including Google & YouTube app)

DSZ (dropshipping app) integration review:

Ensuring correct attributes (no pickup, no returns, delivery timeframe)



Frontend / Store Logic



Theme updates to support “Online Only” messaging

Promo code and discount configuration for DSZ products

Priority Focus Areas



The most critical areas to resolve first:



Local Inventory Feed configuration and accuracy

Shopify to GMC data flow and sync issues

Feed app selection and clean migration



These issues are currently causing GMC errors and confusion around in-store vs online availability.



Ideal Candidate

Strong experience with Shopify and Google Merchant Center

Proven experience with local inventory ads and feed setup

Deep understanding of product feeds and attribute mapping

Experience with feed tools (DataFeedWatch, Multifeeds, or similar)

Experience managing large product catalogs (1,000+ variants)

Comfortable with Shopify APIs, metafields, and automation workflows

Experience with dropshipping integrations is a plus

Application Requirement



Please include a short Loom video with your application covering:



Your experience with Shopify and Google Merchant Center

How you would approach the responsibilities outlined above

How you would diagnose and resolve the current issues (especially local inventory feed and GMC sync)



Applications without a Loom video may not be considered.



Engagement

Project-based with potential for ongoing work

Must be able to audit, diagnose, and implement fixes efficiently

Clear communication and documentation required



If you have hands-on experience with Shopify, GMC, and local inventory setups, we’d like to hear from you.

title: Go To Market Strategist (Saas)
description:
Go-To-Market Strategist

Part-Time | 15 Hours per Week | Remote / Ireland Preferred



About Constituency

Constituency.ie is a civic technology platform designed to modernise how elected representatives manage constituency cases and communicate with citizens. The platform provides a structured, transparent, and secure system for handling both private and public representation cases, petitions, and communications between citizens and representatives.

Our mission is to improve democratic engagement by giving representatives better tools to manage cases while providing citizens with clearer visibility into how issues are being addressed.



We are preparing for our next phase of growth and are seeking a Go-To-Market Strategist to help shape and execute the platform’s market entry and expansion strategy.



Role Overview

The Go-To-Market Strategist will be responsible for developing and guiding the market launch strategy for Constituency.ie. This role focuses on defining the target audience, positioning the product within the political and public sector landscape, and building a structured plan to drive adoption among elected representatives.

This is a strategic role focused on planning, positioning, and early growth initiatives rather than day-to-day marketing execution.



Key Responsibilities



Market Strategy

Develop and refine the go-to-market strategy for Constituency.ie.

Identify the most effective entry points into the political and public sector market.

Define the target customer segments (e.g., councillors, TDs, MPs, political offices).

Create strategies to support early adoption and platform credibility.

Positioning & Messaging

Define the core value proposition for representatives and citizens.

Develop clear messaging around digital constituency management and transparency.

Ensure the platform is positioned as a trusted civic technology solution.



Market Research

Conduct research on:

political workflows

existing case management practices

comparable civic platforms internationally

Identify potential barriers to adoption and recommend strategies to overcome them.

Launch Planning

Develop a structured product launch roadmap.

Identify early adopters and pilot opportunities.

Recommend outreach strategies including:

political networks

representative offices

conferences and associations



Growth Opportunities

Identify potential partnerships with political organisations, advisory groups, and public bodies.

Support the development of pricing and subscription models.

Advise on scalable acquisition strategies.



Ideal Candidate

We are seeking someone who understands market strategy, public sector environments, and early-stage product growth.



Experience

Experience in go-to-market strategy, product strategy, or growth strategy

Experience working with startups, SaaS platforms, or technology products

Knowledge of government, public sector, or political environments is an advantage

Experience launching new products or services



Skills

Strategic thinking and structured planning

Market research and competitive analysis

Strong communication and positioning skills

Ability to translate complex ideas into clear messaging

Comfortable working in an early-stage environment



Working Structure

Part-Time: 15 hours per week

Flexible working hours

Remote role (Ireland or UK preferred)

Collaboration with the founder and core product team

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
Full Stack Developer Job Description



Job Title: Full Stack Developer

Location: Remote

Employment Type: Part-time

Hours Available: 20 Hours

Timezone: Irish Time (GMT) 10am-2pm or 11am-3pm





About the Role

We are looking for a Full Stack Developer to build, improve, and maintain modern web applications from frontend to backend. You will work closely with product, design, and operations teams to ship features quickly, reliably, and securely.





Key Responsibilities

· Build and maintain frontend applications with responsive, accessible UI.



· Develop backend APIs, business logic, and database integrations.



· Design clean, scalable architecture and reusable components.



· Write secure, tested, and maintainable code.



· Optimize application performance, reliability, and developer workflows.



· Collaborate with cross-functional teams to plan and deliver features.



· Review code, mentor teammates, and improve engineering standards.





Required Skills & Experience

· 3+ years experience in full stack development.



· Strong JavaScript/TypeScript skills.



· Experience with a frontend framework (React/Next.js preferred).



· Experience with backend frameworks (Node.js, Python/Django).



· Solid understanding of REST APIs and authentication/authorization.



· Practical knowledge of SQL/relational databases.



· Experience with Git, CI/CD, and modern development workflows.



· Experience working in Agile development environments (sprint planning, task tracking, and iterative delivery).



· Solid understanding of SSDLC standards and secure development practices aligned with ISO 27001.



· Strong debugging, communication, and ownership mindset.



· Experience with AWS cloud platforms.



· Experience with Docker and containerized deployments.





Nice to Have

· Experience integrating payment systems (e.g., Stripe, PayPal) and supporting subscription/billing workflows.



· Familiarity with monitoring/logging tools.



· Experience with unit/integration/e2e testing.



· Experience building SaaS or project management platforms.





Tech Stack (Example)

· Frontend: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, shadcn/ui



· Backend: Python (Django) or Node.js



· Database: PostgreSQL



· Infrastructure: Docker, CI/CD pipelines, cloud hosting





What Success Looks Like

· Features are delivered on time with high quality.



· Bugs and regressions decrease through better test coverage.



· Performance and reliability improve release over release.



· Team velocity improves through clearer architecture and reusable systems.





How to Apply

Please send:

· Updated CV/Resume



· Short cover note



· Portfolio & GitHub links (if available)

title: CLEANUP — 3 YEARS OF MESSY BOOKS IN QUICKBOOKS
description:
URGENT BOOKKEEPING CLEANUP — 3 YEARS OF MESSY BOOKS IN QUICKBOOKS



???? DEADLINE-DRIVEN PROJECT — SERIOUS INQUIRIES ONLY ????



---



OVERVIEW



I'm a small business owner who is approximately 3 years behind on filing my taxes. I need an experienced QuickBooks bookkeeper/accountant to do a full cleanup and categorization of my books so that I can hand everything off to a CPA and get my taxes filed as quickly as possible. This is a large, paid project and I need someone who can start soon.



EST 2000 Transcations



---



THE SITUATION



Over the past ~3 years, I made the mistake of using my business credit/debit card for personal purchases as well. This means my QuickBooks account has a significant mix of:



• Business expenses recorded correctly

• Personal expenses that were run through the business card and are now sitting in the books incorrectly

• Transactions that are miscategorized, uncategorized, or missing entirely

• Accounts that have never been properly reconciled



The end result is that my books are disorganized and unusable in their current state. Before I can hand anything to a CPA to file 2-3 years of back taxes, everything needs to be sorted, cleaned, and properly categorized.



---



WHAT I NEED YOU TO DO



1. Full Transaction Review (3 Years)

   - Go through all transactions across all connected accounts in QuickBooks for approximately the last 3 years

   - Identify and flag all personal transactions that were run through the business

   - Separate business expenses from personal expenses clearly



2. Proper Categorization

   - Categorize all business transactions correctly using standard chart of accounts (e.g., office supplies, software subscriptions, travel, meals, contractor payments, equipment, etc.)

   - Apply consistent, tax-friendly categories that a CPA will recognize immediately

   - Handle any uncategorized or "ask my accountant" items appropriately



3. Account Reconciliation

   - Reconcile all bank and credit card accounts month by month for the full 3-year period

   - Identify and resolve any discrepancies between bank statements and QuickBooks records

   - Flag any missing transactions or duplicates



4. Clean-Up of Owner's Draw / Personal Transactions

   - Properly move all personal expenses out of the business books (owner's draw, shareholder distribution, or equivalent depending on business entity type)

   - Ensure the books reflect only legitimate business activity



5. Profit & Loss / Balance Sheet Clean-Up

   - Make sure P&L and Balance Sheet reports are accurate and presentable for each of the 3 tax years

   - Ensure reports are ready to be pulled and handed directly to a CPA



6. Final Review & Handoff Package

   - Provide a summary of what was done, what was found, and any notes the CPA should be aware of

   - Flag any areas of concern (missing receipts, large unusual transactions, etc.)

   - Deliver clean, organized books ready for tax prep



---



WHAT I'LL PROVIDE YOU



• Access to my QuickBooks account (Online or Desktop — TBD)

• Bank and credit card statements for the 3-year period (PDFs or CSV exports)

• Any receipts or documentation I have available

• Availability to answer questions about specific transactions

• Context about my business type and general expense categories



---



IDEAL CANDIDATE



? Proven experience with QuickBooks Online or Desktop cleanup projects

? Experience handling mixed personal/business transaction cleanup

? Familiarity with small business tax prep and working alongside CPAs

? Detail-oriented, organized, and communicative

? Able to handle 2-3 years of data efficiently

? US-based or highly familiar with US tax categories preferred

? QuickBooks ProAdvisor certification is a major plus



Bonus: Experience with back-tax situations or working under deadline pressure



---



SCOPE & BUDGET



This is a significant project. I'm not looking for a bargain — I'm looking for someone who is genuinely skilled and will get this done correctly the first time. I understand this is a multi-hour, potentially multi-day job and I'm prepared to pay appropriately for quality work.



Please include in your proposal:

• Your estimated timeline to complete the project

• Your rate (flat project fee or hourly)

• Examples of similar cleanup projects you've completed

• Any questions you have about the scope



---



HOW TO APPLY



Please don't send a generic response. Tell me:

1. Have you done a messy books / multi-year cleanup like this before?

2. How do you typically handle personal vs. business transaction separation?

3. What's your estimated timeline for a project of this scope?

4. What's your rate?



I'm moving quickly on this. Serious, qualified applicants only — let's get this done.

title: OPERATIONS MANAGER – TOURISM & HOSPITALITY – PALAWAN (EL NIDO)
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

We are a fast-growing travel and expedition company based in El Nido, Palawan, operating daily tours, private expeditions, and multi-day experiences for international clients.



Our operations involve boats, drivers, guides, logistics teams, and multiple on-site partners, across El Nido, Coron, and Puerto Princesa.



We are now looking for a strong, hands-on Operations Manager to act as the founder’s right hand on the ground - someone who can supervise teams, enforce standards, detect issues early, and ensure operations run reliably every day.



This is not a remote role. This is a field-based leadership position.



OPERATIONS MANAGER – TOURISM & HOSPITALITY – PALAWAN (EL NIDO)

(Field Operations Lead / Founder’s Right Hand)



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



• Supervise daily operations across tours, logistics, and field teams (boats, drivers, guides, coordination staff)

• Be physically present on the ground to monitor execution, verify standards, and detect issues early

• Ensure all bookings, operations data, and reporting are accurate, complete, and reliable

• Identify operational dysfunctions (delays, inconsistencies, poor coordination, missing information) and resolve them quickly

• Detect and address irregularities, leakages, or potential fraud (cash handling, supplier discrepancies, internal shortcuts, etc.)

• Enforce discipline, accountability, and high standards across all teams

• Coordinate with partners and suppliers to ensure smooth and reliable service delivery

• Implement and improve processes, SOPs, and operational structure as the company grows

• Support team leaders, train staff, and ensure alignment across all locations

• Report directly to the founder and act as his eyes and ears on the ground



WHAT YOU BRING



• 5+ years experience in operations within tourism or hospitality (resorts, hotels, tour operators, transport/logistics, marine or fleet operations)

• Proven experience managing on-site teams (not remote) in real operational environments

• Strong ability to enforce rules, maintain discipline, and handle difficult situations

• Experience dealing with operational issues, staff misconduct, or fraud situations

• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, imperfect, real-world environment

• Strong organizational and problem-solving skills

• Fluent in English, and comfortable working with both local teams and international clients

• Willing to be based in El Nido and travel regularly to Coron and Puerto Princesa



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



This role is for someone who is:



• Hands-on and field-oriented — not just behind a computer

• Detail-oriented and highly observant

• Comfortable confronting issues directly and taking decisions

• Able to manage people, not just processes

• Reliable, structured, and consistent in execution

• Looking for a long-term role with responsibility and impact



APPLICATION CHECK



To confirm that you have carefully read this job description, please start your application with the words:



“Standards matter.”



Then answer the following question in 4–6 lines:



If you were responsible for supervising operations in a travel company, what types of dysfunctions or irregularities would you watch for?



Please also attach your CV to your application.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



IMPORTANT



• This role is based in El Nido, Palawan

• This is not a remote position

• Experience in tourism, hospitality, or field operations is required

• Candidates without relevant on-site operational experience will not be considered



WHY YOU WILL LOVE IT



• Key leadership role working directly with the founder

• High level of responsibility and autonomy

• Opportunity to structure and scale real-world operations

• Dynamic, international environment in one of the most beautiful locations in the Philippines

• Strong impact on the growth and performance of the company



LOCATION



El Nido, Palawan

Regular travel to Coron and Puerto Princesa
